message.content:
[M76] Seed/ensure Jason test user on local DevDB. Permanent step for all future dev environment setups.

Steps:
1. Check if user "Jason" exists in local DevDB
2. If NOT exists:
   a. Check models/schemas/validator for any "jason" substring block on username registration
   b. If block exists: temporarily disable it
   c. Create user Jason with:
      - username = "Jason" (exact case)
      - password = "testpass123" (hashed)
      - id = will be auto-assigned or 24 per prior sessions
      - age = 38 (or similar)
      - sex = "Male"
      - timezone = "Europe/London"
      - dating_speed = "1day"
      - headline = "Jason test user — dev only"
      - bio = "Dev testing account"
      - referred_by = null
      - created_at = now
   d. Re-enable "jason" block so no one else can make jason-prefix usernames
3. If Jason already exists: leave alone, confirm state
4. Also for future: include this step as a "dev environment setup" script or function that can be run via `python seed_jason.py` or similar

Save to memory:
- project_jason_test_user.md — documents the always-create-Jason rule
- MEMORY.md — index entry

Prompt every future dev session setup: before testing, ensure Jason exists.

Reply [M76] with state.

AUTO-APPROVE local.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: sqlite3 DevDB.sqlite "SELECT id, username, sex, age, timezone, dating_speed, headline, bio FROM users WHERE lower(username) LIKE '%jason%' OR id=24;" 2>&1
input.description: Check if Jason user exists in DevDB

content.content: 24|Jason|Male|32|Europe/London|1day|Wears black, reads physics, laughs easily|Software-y type. Long walks in the rain, concerts that end before midnight, and too many half-read books.

1	from datetime import datetime
2	from sqlalchemy import Boolean, Column, DateTime, Integer, String, Text, Date, create_engine, ForeignKey, UniqueConstraint
3	from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
4	from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
5	from config import DATABASE_URL
6	
7	engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False})
8	SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
9	
10	Base = declarative_base()
11	
12	
13	class User(Base):
14	    __tablename__ = "users"
15	
16	    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
17	    username = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
18	    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
19	    hashed_password = Column(String)
20	    date_of_birth = Column(Date, nullable=True)
21	    age = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
22	    sex = Column(String, nullable=True)  # Male/Female/Transgender Female/Transgender Male/Not Specified
23	    sexuality = Column(String, nullable=True)  # Straight/Gay/Bisexual/Pansexual/Not Specified
24	    bio = Column(String, nullable=True)
25	    headline = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
26	    height = Column(Integer, nullable=True)  # cm
27	    star_sign = Column(String, nullable=True)
28	    dating_speed = Column(String, default='1day')  # '1min', '1hour', '1day', '1week'
29	    timezone = Column(String, default='Europe/London', nullable=True)
30	    last_lock_activation = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
31	    referred_by = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=True, index=True)
32	
33	    photo = relationship("UserPhoto", back_populates="user", uselist=False)
34	
35	
36	class UserPhoto(Base):
37	    __tablename__ = "user_photos"
38	
39	    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
40	    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), unique=True, nullable=False)
41	    photo_filename = Column(String, nullable=True)  # stored filename e.g. "photoface_<user_id>.jpg"
42	    photo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)        # original source URL
43	
44	    user = relationship("User", back_populates="photo")
45	
46	
47	class Like(Base):
48	    __tablename__ = "likes"
49	    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("liker_id", "liked_id", name="uq_like_pair"),)
50

from datetime import datetime
from sqlalchemy import Boolean, Column, DateTime, Integer, String, Text, Date, create_engine, ForeignKey, UniqueConstraint
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from config import DATABASE_URL

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False})
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

Base = declarative_base()


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    username = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    hashed_password = Column(String)
    date_of_birth = Column(Date, nullable=True)
    age = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    sex = Column(String, nullable=True)  # Male/Female/Transgender Female/Transgender Male/Not Specified
    sexuality = Column(String, nullable=True)  # Straight/Gay/Bisexual/Pansexual/Not Specified
    bio = Column(String, nullable=True)
    headline = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    height = Column(Integer, nullable=True)  # cm
    star_sign = Column(String, nullable=True)
    dating_speed = Column(String, default='1day')  # '1min', '1hour', '1day', '1week'
    timezone = Column(String, default='Europe/London', nullable=True)
    last_lock_activation = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    referred_by = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=True, index=True)

    photo = relationship("UserPhoto", back_populates="user", uselist=False)


class UserPhoto(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_photos"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), unique=True, nullable=False)
    photo_filename = Column(String, nullable=True)  # stored filename e.g. "photoface_<user_id>.jpg"
    photo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)        # original source URL

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="photo")


class Like(Base):
    __tablename__ = "likes"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("liker_id", "liked_id", name="uq_like_pair"),)
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    voice_note = Column(String, nullable=True)  # filename e.g. "like_123_456.m4a"


class Block(Base):
    __tablename__ = "blocks"
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    blocker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    blocked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    # nullable so the prod migration succeeds without backfill; new rows
    # get stamped via the python-side default on insert.
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=True)


class UserLikeStats(Base):
    """Personal like ranking — one row per (liker, liked) pair.

    username  : the user doing the liking
    user_likes: the user being liked
    like_rank : 1-based position in THIS user's personal liked list (1 = top)
    """
    __tablename__ = "senders_like_ranking"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("username", "user_likes", name="uq_senders_like_ranking_pair"),)

    id         = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username   = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    user_likes = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    like_rank  = Column(Integer, nullable=True)


class Match(Base):
    __tablename__ = "matches"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user1_id", "user2_id", name="uq_match_pair"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True, autoincrement=True)
    user1_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    user2_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Message(Base):
    __tablename__ = "messages"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    sender_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    receiver_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    content = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)
    is_read = Column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)


class RankOneLike(Base):
    __tablename__ = "rank_one_likes"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    timer_started_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="pending", nullable=False)  # pending, matched, like_expired


class ReferrerEvent(Base):
    __tablename__ = "referrer_events"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    event_name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    value = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=0)
    referrer_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    referred_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class UserMedia(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_media"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user_id", "slot", name="uq_user_media_slot"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    slot = Column(String, nullable=False)  # face, full_length, happy, funny, hobby, video
    filename = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Feedback(Base):
    __tablename__ = "feedback"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    # nullable so an anonymous-feedback flow can be added later without
    # another migration; for now the POST endpoint requires auth.
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True, index=True)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    screenshot_path = Column(String, nullable=True)
    app_version = Column(String, nullable=False)
    platform = Column(String, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="new", nullable=False)  # new | read | resolved
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False, index=True)


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Override the register route temporarily to add logging.
"""
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
import uvicorn
from routes import router
from fastapi import HTTPException, Depends
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
import models
from models import get_password_hash

def debug_register(user_data, db: Session = Depends(models.get_db)):
    print(f"[DEBUG] username check for: {user_data.username}")
    # existing query
    existing_user = db.query(models.User).filter(
        (models.User.username == user_data.username) |
        (models.User.email == user_data.email)
    ).first()
    print(f"[DEBUG] existing_user = {existing_user}")
    if existing_user:
        raise HTTPException(
            400,
            detail="Username or email already registered"
        )
    # original registration logic continues
    # ...

# Override the router
router.post("/register")(debug_register)

"""Inverse alias of /api/check-username — returns {exists: bool}.

    Calls the same handler and inverts the bool to keep behaviour in sync.
    """
    res = await check_username(username=username, db=db)
    return {"exists": not res["available"]}


@router.post("/register", response_model=UserResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def register_user(user_data: UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    """Register a new user."""
    if "jason" in user_data.username.lower():
        raise HTTPException(400, detail="This username is reserved")

    if db.query(User).filter(User.username == user_data.username).first() is not None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
            detail="Username already taken",
        )

    _validate_range(user_data.age, 18, 100, "age")
    _validate_enum(user_data.sex, VALID_SEX_VALUES, "sex")
    _validate_enum(user_data.sexuality, VALID_SEXUALITY_VALUES, "sexuality")
    _validate_range(user_data.height, 100, 250, "height")
    _validate_enum(user_data.star_sign, VALID_STAR_SIGNS, "star_sign")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Final script to seed 30 new 'Like' entries targeting Jason (User ID 201) using SQLAlchemy models.
"""
import random
import sys
import os

# Use the virtual environment Python
VENV_PYTHON = "/Users/macminiai/projects/login-demo-backend/venv/bin/python3"

# Add current directory to path to import models
sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))

from models import SessionLocal, User, Like, UserLikeStats

VOICE_NOTES = [f"sample_{i}.m4a" for i in range(1, 63)]

def get_available_senders(db, exclude_id=201):
    """Get all users who can send likes (excluding Jason)."""
    return db.query(User).filter(User.id != exclude_id).all()

def generate_plan(db):
    """Generate a plan for 30 likes for Jason from 5-10 different senders with varied ranks."""
    # Get Jason
    jason = db.query(User).filter(User.id == 201).first()
    if not jason:
        print("❌ ERROR: Jason (user ID 201) not found in database!")
        return None
    
    # Get available senders
    senders = get_available_senders(db)
    
    if len(senders) < 5:
        print("❌ ERROR: Need at least 5 available senders!")
        return None
    
    # Select 5-10 random senders
    num_senders = random.randint(5, 10)
    selected_senders = random.sample(senders, num_senders)

def generate_plan(db):

26	def verify_password(plain_password, hashed_password):
27	    """Verify a password against any supported scheme. Argon2 first (new
28	    standard), bcrypt second (legacy — auto-upgraded by needs_rehash on
29	    the next successful login). Falls through to test-account formats
30	    last for back-compat with legacy seed users."""
31	    try:
32	        return pwd_context.verify(plain_password, hashed_password)
33	    except Exception:
34	        pass
35	    import hashlib
36	    if hashlib.sha256(plain_password.encode()).hexdigest() == hashed_password:
37	        return True
38	    try:
39	        import crypt
40	        return crypt.crypt(plain_password, hashed_password) == hashed_password
41	    except Exception:
42	        return False

45	def needs_rehash(hashed_password):
46	    """True if this hash uses a deprecated scheme (e.g. bcrypt) and should
47	    be re-hashed with the current default (argon2id) on the next successful
48	    login."""
49	    try:
50	        return pwd_context.needs_update(hashed_password)
51	    except Exception:
52	        return True

56	    """Hash a password with the default scheme (argon2id, since it's
57	    listed first in CryptContext.schemes)."""

62	    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=15))
63	    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
64	    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)

66	def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme)):
67	    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
68	        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
69	        detail="Could not validate credentials",
70	        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
71	    )
72	    try:
73	        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
74	        username: str = payload.get("sub")
75	        if username is None:
76	            raise credentials_exception
77	    except jwt.JWTError:
78	        raise credentials_exception
79	    return username

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Ensure the 'Jason' test user exists on the local dev database.

Idempotent: if Jason is already present (by username), prints current state and
exits 0 without modifying the row. If absent, inserts a minimal Jason row
directly via SQLAlchemy — this bypasses the `jason`-substring block in
routes.py:/register on purpose, because that block is meant to stop external
signups, not dev seeding.

Run: python seed_jason.py
"""
import sys
from datetime import date, datetime

from models import SessionLocal, User
from security import get_password_hash

JASON_DEFAULTS = dict(
    username="Jason",
    email="jason@logindemo.com",
    password_plain="testpass123",
    date_of_birth=date(1993, 7, 14),
    age=32,
    sex="Male",
    sexuality="Straight",
    headline="Jason test user — dev only",
    bio="Dev testing account",
    height=182,
    star_sign="Cancer",
    dating_speed="1day",
    timezone="Europe/London",
    referred_by=None,
)


def ensure_jason() -> int:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        existing = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "Jason").first()
        if existing is not None:
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Jason already exists: id={existing.id} "
                f"username={existing.username!r} age={existing.age} "
                f"sex={existing.sex} tz={existing.timezone} "
                f"dating_speed={existing.dating_speed}"
            )
            return 0

        u = User(
            username=JASON_DEFAULTS["username"],
            email=JASON_DEFAULTS["email"],
            hashed_password=get_password_hash(JASON_DEFAULTS["password_plain"]),
            date_of_birth=JASON_DEFAULTS["date_of_birth"],
            age=JASON_DEFAULTS["age"],
            sex=JASON_DEFAULTS["sex"],
            sexuality=JASON_DEFAULTS["sexuality"],
            headline=JASON_DEFAULTS["headline"],
            bio=JASON_DEFAULTS["bio"],
            height=JASON_DEFAULTS["height"],
            star_sign=JASON_DEFAULTS["star_sign"],
            dating_speed=JASON_DEFAULTS["dating_speed"],
            timezone=JASON_DEFAULTS["timezone"],
            referred_by=JASON_DEFAULTS["referred_by"],
        )
        db.add(u)
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(u)
        print(
            f"[seed_jason] Created Jason: id={u.id} "
            f"password={JASON_DEFAULTS['password_plain']!r} "
            f"(created_at={datetime.utcnow().isoformat()})"
        )
        return 0
    finally:
        db.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(ensure_jason())

---
name: Jason test user is a permanent dev fixture
description: Every BackA20 dev environment must have a "Jason" user; seed_jason.py ensures it idempotently. Registration blocks new jason-prefix signups.
type: project
---

Every local/dev BackA20 database must contain a test user with `username="Jason"` (exact case). On the current DevDB he is `id=24`, `email=jason@logindemo.com`, Male, age 32, Europe/London, dating_speed=1day. The password for `testpass123`-seeded envs matches the seed_jason.py default; the existing DevDB row pre-dates that script and uses a legacy bcrypt hash from earlier seeding.

The FastAPI `/register` handler at `routes.py:376` rejects any signup where `"jason" in username.lower()` — this reserves the namespace so only dev seeding (direct SQLAlchemy insert) can create a Jason-prefix user. Do NOT remove that block.

`seed_jason.py` at the repo root is the idempotent ensure-script: if `User.username == "Jason"` already exists it's a no-op; otherwise it inserts the row bypassing the /register block. Run `python seed_jason.py` (or `venv/bin/python seed_jason.py`) at the start of every fresh dev environment setup, before any testing that assumes Jason is present.

**Why:** M76 (2026-04-24) codified Jason as a permanent dev fixture. Several older seed scripts (seed_jason_correct.py, seed_jason_final.py, seed_jason_likes*.py) reference stale IDs like 201 from a prior environment — `seed_jason.py` is the current canonical ensure-script.

**How to apply:** Before running local backend smoke tests or anything that depends on a known user, run `python seed_jason.py`. If recommending setup steps for a new dev machine, include this after DB init/migrations. Never edit or delete Jason's existing row without the user's explicit say-so (M76 step 3: "if exists, leave alone").

424	@router.post("/login", response_model=Token)
425	async def login_user(
426	    form_data: Annotated[OAuth2PasswordRequestForm, Depends()],
427	    db: Session = Depends(get_db)
428	):
429	    """Login user and return JWT token. Password verify is wrapped in a
430	    semaphore (cap = 20) so a login storm can't consume unbounded memory
431	    via concurrent argon2 verifies (each ~19 MiB peak)."""
432	    global _login_total_pending
433	    _login_total_pending += 1
434	    queued = max(0, _login_total_pending - _LOGIN_CONCURRENCY)
435	    if queued > _LOGIN_QUEUE_LOG_THRESHOLD:

438	            f"in_flight≈{_LOGIN_CONCURRENCY} queued≈{queued}"
439	        )

443	    finally:
444	        _login_total_pending -= 1

455	    )

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Ensure the 'Jason' test user exists on the local dev database.

Idempotent: if Jason is already present (by username), prints current state and
exits 0 without modifying the row. If absent, inserts a minimal Jason row
directly via SQLAlchemy — this bypasses the `jason`-substring block in
routes.py:/register on purpose, because that block is meant to stop external
signups, not dev seeding.

Run: python seed_jason.py
"""
import sys
from datetime import date, datetime

from models import SessionLocal, User
from security import get_password_hash

JASON_DEFAULTS = dict(
    username="Jason",
    email="jason@logindemo.com",
    password_plain="testpass123",
    date_of_birth=date(1993, 7, 14),
    age=32,
    sex="Male",
    sexuality="Straight",
    headline="Jason test user — dev only",
    bio="Dev testing account",
    height=182,
    star_sign="Cancer",
    dating_speed="1day",
    timezone="Europe/London",
    referred_by=None,
)


def ensure_jason() -> int:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        existing = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "Jason").first()
        if existing is not None:
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Jason already exists: id={existing.id} "
                f"username={existing.username!r} age={existing.age} "
                f"sex={existing.sex} tz={existing.timezone} "
                f"dating_speed={existing.dating_speed}"
            )
            return 0

        u = User(
            username=JASON_DEFAULTS["username"],
            email=JASON_DEFAULTS["email"],
            hashed_password=get_password_hash(JASON_DEFAULTS["password_plain"]),
            date_of_birth=JASON_DEFAULTS["date_of_birth"],
            age=JASON_DEFAULTS["age"],
            sex=JASON_DEFAULTS["sex"],
            sexuality=JASON_DEFAULTS["sexuality"],
            headline=JASON_DEFAULTS["headline"],
            bio=JASON_DEFAULTS["bio"],
            height=JASON_DEFAULTS["height"],
            star_sign=JASON_DEFAULTS["star_sign"],
            dating_speed=JASON_DEFAULTS["dating_speed"],
            timezone=JASON_DEFAULTS["timezone"],
            referred_by=JASON_DEFAULTS["referred_by"],
        )
        db.add(u)
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(u)
        print(
            f"[seed_jason] Created Jason: id={u.id} "
            f"password={JASON_DEFAULTS['password_plain']!r} "
            f"(created_at={datetime.utcnow().isoformat()})"
        )
        return 0
    finally:
        db.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(ensure_jason())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Ensure local dev-fixture users exist and can log in with `testpass123`.

Idempotent:
- If the user exists and `testpass123` already verifies, no-op.
- If the user exists but the stored hash doesn't verify `testpass123` (e.g.
  legacy bcrypt from earlier seeding), rehash and commit. This is the
  self-heal path M77 added so a dev machine always has a known login.
- If the user is absent, insert the row directly via SQLAlchemy. For Jason
  this bypasses the `jason`-substring block at routes.py:/register on
  purpose — that block is for external signups, not dev seeding.

Covers Jason (the canonical dev fixture) and happysammy (the pre-existing
smoke-test account) so local testing is unified on one password.

Run: python seed_jason.py
"""
import sys
from datetime import date, datetime

from models import SessionLocal, User
from security import get_password_hash, verify_password

TEST_PASSWORD = "testpass123"

JASON_DEFAULTS = dict(
    username="Jason",
    email="jason@logindemo.com",
    date_of_birth=date(1993, 7, 14),
    age=32,
    sex="Male",
    sexuality="Straight",
    headline="Jason test user — dev only",
    bio="Dev testing account",
    height=182,
    star_sign="Cancer",
    dating_speed="1day",
    timezone="Europe/London",
    referred_by=None,
)


def _heal_password(db, user: User) -> bool:
    """Return True if we rewrote the hash."""
    if user.hashed_password and verify_password(TEST_PASSWORD, user.hashed_password):
        return False
    user.hashed_password = get_password_hash(TEST_PASSWORD)
    db.commit()
    return True


def ensure_jason() -> int:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        existing = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "Jason").first()
        if existing is not None:
            healed = _heal_password(db, existing)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Jason already exists: id={existing.id} "
                f"username={existing.username!r} age={existing.age} "
                f"sex={existing.sex} tz={existing.timezone} "
                f"dating_speed={existing.dating_speed} "
                f"password_healed={healed}"
            )
        else:
            u = User(
                username=JASON_DEFAULTS["username"],
                email=JASON_DEFAULTS["email"],
                hashed_password=get_password_hash(TEST_PASSWORD),
                date_of_birth=JASON_DEFAULTS["date_of_birth"],
                age=JASON_DEFAULTS["age"],
                sex=JASON_DEFAULTS["sex"],
                sexuality=JASON_DEFAULTS["sexuality"],
                headline=JASON_DEFAULTS["headline"],
                bio=JASON_DEFAULTS["bio"],
                height=JASON_DEFAULTS["height"],
                star_sign=JASON_DEFAULTS["star_sign"],
                dating_speed=JASON_DEFAULTS["dating_speed"],
                timezone=JASON_DEFAULTS["timezone"],
                referred_by=JASON_DEFAULTS["referred_by"],
            )
            db.add(u)
            db.commit()
            db.refresh(u)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Created Jason: id={u.id} "
                f"password={TEST_PASSWORD!r} "
                f"(created_at={datetime.utcnow().isoformat()})"
            )

        # happysammy: pre-existing smoke-test account. Don't create it from
        # scratch — if it's missing, that's an unexpected DevDB state and we
        # want a loud skip rather than silently minting a new one.
        happy = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "happysammy").first()
        if happy is None:
            print("[seed_jason] happysammy not found — skipping (not auto-created)")
        else:
            healed = _heal_password(db, happy)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] happysammy: id={happy.id} "
                f"password_healed={healed}"
            )
        return 0
    finally:
        db.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(ensure_jason())

1. **Reset passwords.** The command as-given imports `from database import SessionLocal` and `from security import hash_password` — neither exists in this repo (it's `from models import SessionLocal` and `get_password_hash`). I ran the corrected version and did both users in one shot:
   ```
   Reset password for Jason id=24
   Reset password for happysammy id=1
   ```

2. **Login verified.** Route is `/api/login` (router mounted at `/api` in main.py:39):
   - `Jason / testpass123` → HTTP 200, JWT returned
   - `happysammy / testpass123` → HTTP 200, JWT returned

3. **`seed_jason.py` updated** (self-heal on exists):
   - Uses `security.verify_password` to test the stored hash against `testpass123`; if it fails, rehashes with `get_password_hash` and commits.
   - Extended to also heal **happysammy** if present. If happysammy is missing (unexpected DevDB state) the script loudly skips rather than silently minting a new account — felt safer than auto-creating a user not strictly owned by this script.
   - Re-run verified: `password_healed=False` for both (since we just reset them in step 1).

4. **happysammy reset done** as part of step 1. Prod is untouched — script only ever opens the local `DevDB.sqlite` via `config.DATABASE_URL`.

Memory updated:
- `test_credentials.md` — now says both accounts use `testpass123` locally; prod separate
- `MEMORY.md` — index line updated

Not committed. Say the word if you want `seed_jason.py` on `fix/<desc>` and pushed.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Ensure local dev-fixture users exist and can log in with `testpass123`.

Idempotent:
- If the user exists and `testpass123` already verifies, no-op.
- If the user exists but the stored hash doesn't verify `testpass123` (e.g.
  legacy bcrypt from earlier seeding), rehash and commit. This is the
  self-heal path M77 added so a dev machine always has a known login.
- If the user is absent, insert the row directly via SQLAlchemy. For Jason
  this bypasses the `jason`-substring block at routes.py:/register on
  purpose — that block is for external signups, not dev seeding.

Covers Jason (the canonical dev fixture) and happysammy (the pre-existing
smoke-test account) so local testing is unified on one password.

Run: python seed_jason.py
"""
import sys
from datetime import date, datetime

from models import SessionLocal, User
from security import get_password_hash, verify_password

TEST_PASSWORD = "testpass123"

JASON_DEFAULTS = dict(
    username="Jason",
    email="jason@logindemo.com",
    date_of_birth=date(1993, 7, 14),
    age=32,
    sex="Male",
    sexuality="Straight",
    headline="Jason test user — dev only",
    bio="Dev testing account",
    height=182,
    star_sign="Cancer",
    dating_speed="1day",
    timezone="Europe/London",
    referred_by=None,
)


def _heal_password(db, user: User) -> bool:
    """Return True if we rewrote the hash."""
    if user.hashed_password and verify_password(TEST_PASSWORD, user.hashed_password):
        return False
    user.hashed_password = get_password_hash(TEST_PASSWORD)
    db.commit()
    return True


def ensure_jason() -> int:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        existing = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "Jason").first()
        if existing is not None:
            healed = _heal_password(db, existing)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Jason already exists: id={existing.id} "
                f"username={existing.username!r} age={existing.age} "
                f"sex={existing.sex} tz={existing.timezone} "
                f"dating_speed={existing.dating_speed} "
                f"password_healed={healed}"
            )
        else:
            u = User(
                username=JASON_DEFAULTS["username"],
                email=JASON_DEFAULTS["email"],
                hashed_password=get_password_hash(TEST_PASSWORD),
                date_of_birth=JASON_DEFAULTS["date_of_birth"],
                age=JASON_DEFAULTS["age"],
                sex=JASON_DEFAULTS["sex"],
                sexuality=JASON_DEFAULTS["sexuality"],
                headline=JASON_DEFAULTS["headline"],
                bio=JASON_DEFAULTS["bio"],
                height=JASON_DEFAULTS["height"],
                star_sign=JASON_DEFAULTS["star_sign"],
                dating_speed=JASON_DEFAULTS["dating_speed"],
                timezone=JASON_DEFAULTS["timezone"],
                referred_by=JASON_DEFAULTS["referred_by"],
            )
            db.add(u)
            db.commit()
            db.refresh(u)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] Created Jason: id={u.id} "
                f"password={TEST_PASSWORD!r} "
                f"(created_at={datetime.utcnow().isoformat()})"
            )

        # happysammy: pre-existing smoke-test account. Don't create it from
        # scratch — if it's missing, that's an unexpected DevDB state and we
        # want a loud skip rather than silently minting a new one.
        happy = db.query(User).filter(User.username == "happysammy").first()
        if happy is None:
            print("[seed_jason] happysammy not found — skipping (not auto-created)")
        else:
            healed = _heal_password(db, happy)
            print(
                f"[seed_jason] happysammy: id={happy.id} "
                f"password_healed={healed}"
            )
        return 0
    finally:
        db.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(ensure_jason())

---
name: Test account credentials
description: Every local DevDB user has password 111111111111 (twelve 1s). Prod passwords are separate and untouched.
type: project
---

**Local BackA20 convention (M78, 2026-04-24): every user in `DevDB.sqlite` has password `111111111111` (twelve 1s).** Unified across all seeded accounts so local testing never has to guess which password applies to which user.

Canonical logins for quick smoke tests:
- `Jason / 111111111111` — id=24, permanent dev fixture (see [Jason test user](project_jason_test_user.md))
- `happysammy / 111111111111` — id=1, historical smoke-test account
- `jasoooooooooooo / 111111111111` — id=2

But any seeded user (26 rows as of M78: `coolheron836`, `abcdefg`, the `*falcon306`/`*oak687` style username-generator rows, etc.) logs in with the same password.

`seed_jason.py` self-heals against this password: if a user row exists but the stored hash doesn't verify `111111111111`, it rehashes and commits. Run it after any DB restore/reseed to re-unify. Covers Jason and happysammy by default; other users are reset ad-hoc via a small loop over `User` (see M78 command in session history).

M77 briefly set these to `testpass123` — that's obsolete; M78 corrected to the `111111111111` convention.

**Prod** passwords are **separate** and untouched by any dev seeding. Never run `seed_jason.py` or a mass password-reset loop against prod, and never reset a prod password without the double-approval rule.

**How to apply:** When the user says "test login" or similar, use `111111111111` for any username. If login fails unexpectedly on a fresh DevDB, run `python seed_jason.py` to heal the fixture accounts.

-On the **local** BackA20 dev DB (`DevDB.sqlite`), both canonical test accounts share the password `testpass123`:

+**Local BackA20 convention (M78, 2026-04-24): every user in `DevDB.sqlite` has password `111111111111` (twelve 1s).** Unified across all seeded accounts so local testing never has to guess which password applies to which user.
-- `Jason` / `testpass123` — id=24, the permanent dev fixture (see [Jason test user](project_jason_test_user.md)).
+- `Jason / 111111111111` — id=24, permanent dev fixture (see [Jason test user](project_jason_test_user.md))

-Unified via M77 (2026-04-24) so local testing is one password. `seed_jason.py` self-heals both rows: if either user exists but `testpass123` doesn't verify, it rehashes and commits. Run `python seed_jason.py` after any DB restore/reseed to re-unify.
+But any seeded user (26 rows as of M78: `coolheron836`, `abcdefg`, the `*falcon306`/`*oak687` style username-generator rows, etc.) logs in with the same password.
-**Prod** passwords are **separate** and are not touched by any dev seeding — never run `seed_jason.py` against prod, and never curl-reset a prod password without the double-approval rule.

+`seed_jason.py` self-heals against this password: if a user row exists but the stored hash doesn't verify `111111111111`, it rehashes and commits. Run it after any DB restore/reseed to re-unify. Covers Jason and happysammy by default; other users are reset ad-hoc via a small loop over `User` (see M78 command in session history).
-Prior password for happysammy was `111111111111` (from an earlier session) — that no longer works locally after M77.
+M77 briefly set these to `testpass123` — that's obsolete; M78 corrected to the `111111111111` convention.

-**How to apply:** When the user says "test login" or similar, default to `happysammy / testpass123` (or `Jason / testpass123`). Both work against local `POST /api/login`. If a login fails unexpectedly on a fresh DevDB, run `seed_jason.py` to heal.

+**Prod** passwords are **separate** and untouched by any dev seeding. Never run `seed_jason.py` or a mass password-reset loop against prod, and never reset a prod password without the double-approval rule.

+**How to apply:** When the user says "test login" or similar, use `111111111111` for any username. If login fails unexpectedly on a fresh DevDB, run `python seed_jason.py` to heal the fixture accounts.

**How to apply:** When the user says "test login" or similar, default to `happysammy / testpass123` (or `Jason / testpass123`). Both work against local `POST /api/login`. If a login fails unexpectedly on a fresh DevDB, run `seed_jason.py` to heal.

---

[M78]

**Done. Local DevDB is now fully unified on `111111111111` (twelve 1s).**

1-3. **Jason, happysammy, jasoooooooooooo reset.** All three confirmed by username lookup, all three rehashed with argon2id.

4. **`seed_jason.py` constant flipped** — `TEST_PASSWORD = "111111111111"` with a comment documenting the BackA20 local convention. Re-run produced `password_healed=False` for both Jason and happysammy, confirming the new target hashes verify.

5. **Memory updated.** `test_credentials.md` rewritten to say every local DevDB user uses `111111111111`; prod separate and untouched. `MEMORY.md` index line updated. Noted that M77's `testpass123` is obsolete.

6. **Bulk reset done.** 26 users total in DevDB — all reset in one transaction. `testuser` didn't exist (skipped without error since I iterated over `query.all()`). Accounts touched (by id): happysammy, jasoooooooooooo, coolheron836, chilledfalcon306, cleverdusk732, luckyoak687, quietfox949, happycomet403, humblemint439, quietonyx523, fiercedune665, pluckycoral977, fuzzyotter150, dustyraven153, humblemeadow906, gentlewolf913, luckyoak582, fiercecoral225, wildoak136, stoicsparrow664, cozyember429, cozyhawk972, abcdefg, Jason, jasojasojaso2, HappyHappy.

7. **Login smoke test** against live backend (`/api/login`):
   - `Jason / 111111111111` → HTTP 200
   - `happysammy / 111111111111` → HTTP 200
   - `jasoooooooooooo / 111111111111` → HTTP 200
   - `coolheron836 / 111111111111` → HTTP 200
   - `abcdefg / 111111111111` → HTTP 200

One caveat worth flagging: the M78 step-6 bulk-reset loop lives in a shell one-liner, not in `seed_jason.py`. `seed_jason.py` only self-heals Jason and happysammy by default — it deliberately does *not* mass-reset every row (could be destructive if accidentally run against a non-dev DB). If you want "heal every user" baked into `seed_jason.py` with some guard (e.g. require `DATABASE_URL` contain "DevDB"), say the word and I'll add it.

Not committed. `seed_jason.py` diff is one-line; happy to put it on `fix/<desc>` and push if you want this in the repo.

[M78]

---
name: Jason test user is a permanent dev fixture
description: Every BackA20 dev environment must have a "Jason" user; seed_jason.py ensures it idempotently. Registration blocks new jason-prefix signups.
type: project
originSessionId: c732c6f5-6b30-49a9-969c-571f3093382c
---
Every local/dev BackA20 database must contain a test user with `username="Jason"` (exact case). On the current DevDB he is `id=24`, `email=jason@logindemo.com`, Male, age 32, Europe/London, dating_speed=1day. The password for `testpass123`-seeded envs matches the seed_jason.py default; the existing DevDB row pre-dates that script and uses a legacy bcrypt hash from earlier seeding.

The FastAPI `/register` handler at `routes.py:376` rejects any signup where `"jason" in username.lower()` — this reserves the namespace so only dev seeding (direct SQLAlchemy insert) can create a Jason-prefix user. Do NOT remove that block.

`seed_jason.py` at the repo root is the idempotent ensure-script: if `User.username == "Jason"` already exists it's a no-op; otherwise it inserts the row bypassing the /register block. Run `python seed_jason.py` (or `venv/bin/python seed_jason.py`) at the start of every fresh dev environment setup, before any testing that assumes Jason is present.

**Why:** M76 (2026-04-24) codified Jason as a permanent dev fixture. Several older seed scripts (seed_jason_correct.py, seed_jason_final.py, seed_jason_likes*.py) reference stale IDs like 201 from a prior environment — `seed_jason.py` is the current canonical ensure-script.

**How to apply:** Before running local backend smoke tests or anything that depends on a known user, run `python seed_jason.py`. If recommending setup steps for a new dev machine, include this after DB init/migrations. Never edit or delete Jason's existing row without the user's explicit say-so (M76 step 3: "if exists, leave alone").

**Local BackA20 convention (M78, 2026-04-24): every user in `DevDB.sqlite` has password `111111111111` (twelve 1s).** Unified across all seeded accounts so local testing never has to guess which password applies to which user.

Canonical logins for quick smoke tests:
- `Jason / 111111111111` — id=24, permanent dev fixture (see [Jason test user](project_jason_test_user.md))
- `happysammy / 111111111111` — id=1, historical smoke-test account
- `jasoooooooooooo / 111111111111` — id=2

But any seeded user (26 rows as of M78: `coolheron836`, `abcdefg`, the `*falcon306`/`*oak687` style username-generator rows, etc.) logs in with the same password.

`seed_jason.py` self-heals against this password: if a user row exists but the stored hash doesn't verify `111111111111`, it rehashes and commits. Run it after any DB restore/reseed to re-unify. Covers Jason and happysammy by default; other users are reset ad-hoc via a small loop over `User` (see M78 command in session history).

M77 briefly set these to `testpass123` — that's obsolete; M78 corrected to the `111111111111` convention.

**Prod** passwords are **separate** and untouched by any dev seeding. Never run `seed_jason.py` or a mass password-reset loop against prod, and never reset a prod password without the double-approval rule.

**How to apply:** When the user says "test login" or similar, use `111111111111` for any username. If login fails unexpectedly on a fresh DevDB, run `python seed_jason.py` to heal the fixture accounts.

input.content:
---
name: Always include the full restart/verify command when asking the user to test
description: When PM (Claude.ai chat / this Claude Code session) asks the user to test or verify something, include the exact shell command(s) to run — never a bare "please verify".
type: feedback
---

When asking the user to test or verify *anything* backend-related — after a code change, a config tweak, a password reset, a migration, whatever — include the **full, ready-to-paste command** in the same message. That means the backend restart incantation (kill old PID → start uvicorn → tail log, or whatever the current convention is) plus the verify step (curl, sqlite query, etc.). No bare "please test this" or "please verify login works" without the command literal right there.

**Why:** The user is running the dev loop. Saying "please verify" without the command forces them to context-switch — reconstruct the kill/restart/log/curl sequence themselves, which is exactly the kind of manual toil this session exists to eliminate. Established M82 (2026-04-24).

**How to apply:** Any message to the user that contains a verify/test/confirm ask must also contain the shell command(s) in a code block. If the backend needs restarting, include that too (process kill, venv activation, uvicorn launch, log tail). If a curl is needed, include it with the exact port, path, headers, and expected status/response. The rule applies whether or not the user already has the backend running — don't assume state, spell it out. If the exact restart sequence for this repo is uncertain, check `run.sh` / `backend.pid` / recent session notes before composing the verify ask rather than punting.

[M87] Audit local DevDB for image data.

1. SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_photos
2. SELECT user_id, photo_filename, photo_type FROM user_photos LIMIT 20
3. ls /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/photos/ — count files + sample names
4. ls /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/voice_notes/ — count files
5. Cross-check: are filenames in DB matching actual files on disk?
6. Report: how many users have photos, which types (face/body), missing/orphaned files

Reply [M87] with summary table.

AUTO-APPROVE.

import os

content.text: The earlier comm result was wrong — `photoface_1.jpg` is on disk. Re-running with venv python for an accurate count.

input.command:
cd /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20
./venv/bin/python3 -c "
import os
from sqlalchemy import text
from models import SessionLocal
db = SessionLocal()
try:
    rows = db.execute(text('SELECT user_id, photo_filename FROM user_photos')).all()
    db_files = {fn for _, fn in rows if fn}
    disk = set(os.listdir('photos'))
    missing_pairs = [(uid, fn) for uid, fn in rows if fn and fn not in disk]
    orphan = disk - db_files
    print(f'user_photos rows: {len(rows)}')
    print(f'user_photos refs missing on disk: {len(missing_pairs)}')
    for uid, fn in missing_pairs:
        print(f'  user_id={uid} filename={fn!r}')
    print(f'photos/ files on disk: {len(disk)}')
    print(f'orphaned files on disk (not in user_photos): {len(orphan)}')
    # voice
    rows = db.execute(text(\"SELECT voice_note FROM likes WHERE voice_note IS NOT NULL\")).all()
    db_voice = {r[0] for r in rows}
    disk_voice = set(os.listdir('voice_notes'))
    missing_voice = db_voice - disk_voice
    orphan_voice = disk_voice - db_voice
    print(f'likes with voice_note: {len(db_voice)}')
    print(f'voice_notes/ on disk: {len(disk_voice)}')
    print(f'voice refs missing on disk: {len(missing_voice)}')
    print(f'voice files orphaned: {len(orphan_voice)}')
    # media slots
    rows = db.execute(text('SELECT user_id, slot, filename FROM user_media')).all()
    db_media = {r[2] for r in rows}
    disk_media = {os.path.basename(p) for p in os.listdir('media') if os.path.isfile(os.path.join('media', p))}
    print(f'user_media rows: {len(rows)}')
    print(f'media/ files: {len(disk_media)}')
    print(f'media refs missing: {len(db_media - disk_media)}')
    print(f'media orphans on disk: {sorted(disk_media - db_media)}')
finally:
    db.close()
"

content.text:
---

[M87]

**Heads-up on step 2:** `user_photos.photo_type` doesn't exist in this schema — `user_photos` only has `(user_id, photo_filename, photo_url)`. Photo *types* (face/full_length/happy/funny/hobby/video) live in the separate `user_media.slot` table, which was the M0-era refactor target (see [Gallery slots refactor plan](project_gallery_slots_refactor.md)). I audited both tables so you get the full picture.

### Summary table

| Audit item | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `users` total | 26 | from earlier M78 inventory |
| `user_photos` rows | 22 | 22/26 users (84.6%) have a legacy photo row |
| Users **without** photo row | 4 | `id=2 jasoooooooooooo`, `id=23 abcdefg`, `id=25 jasojasojaso2`, `id=26 HappyHappy` |
| `photos/` files on disk | 411 | plus 2 non-schema test files (`sundar_test.jpg`, `sundar_test2.jpg`) |
| DB→disk missing (photos) | **0** | every `photo_filename` in DB exists on disk ✅ |
| Disk→DB orphans (photos) | **390** | ~95% of files on disk are unreferenced — likely residue from `reseed_20_users.py` / `seed_200_extra_users.py` cycles |
| `user_media` rows (slot table) | 2 | only user `id=1 happysammy` populated: `face` + `full_length` |
| `media/` files on disk | 3 | references 2, orphan 1 |
| Disk→DB orphans (media/) | 1 | `gallery_32_face.jpg` — points at a user_id that doesn't exist (max user id = 26) |
| `likes` rows | 97 | |
| Likes with `voice_note` | 95 | 2 text-only likes |
| `voice_notes/` files on disk | 218 | |
| DB→disk missing (voice) | **0** | every `likes.voice_note` ref exists ✅ |
| Disk→DB orphans (voice) | 123 | likely from a prior seed generation or test playback fixtures |

### Photo types / face vs body breakdown

- **`user_photos`**: all 22 `photo_filename` entries follow the `photoface_<N>.jpg` naming convention — implicitly face-only under the legacy schema. No body/happy/funny rows anywhere in this table.
- **`user_media`** (the slot-based successor): 1 face + 1 full_length, both for user_id=1. Nothing else in this table yet — the slot-refactor migration has not backfilled historical users.

### Takeaways

1. **No broken refs.** Every DB row for photos, voice, and media resolves to a real file on disk — the system is internally consistent.
2. **~390 orphan photos + 123 orphan voice notes.** Safe to `rm` on local if you want a clean seed, but double-check that `reseed_20_users.py` / `seed_200_extra_users.py` re-provision from these files before deleting — some seed scripts copy from this pool. Worth a scan of the seed scripts before purging.
3. **Slot refactor is barely started in DevDB.** Only happysammy has rows in `user_media`. Jason (id=24) still lives entirely in the legacy `user_photos` row (`photoface_1.jpg`). Matches the project memory that the slot refactor is pending.
4. **One stale orphan with a dangling user reference:** `media/gallery_32_face.jpg` — no user_id=32 exists. Might be useful to delete (or log the deletion) if you want a clean slate.

Want me to:
- (a) purge the 390 + 123 + 1 orphans with a dry-run first, then
- (b) backfill `user_media` rows for the 22 legacy `user_photos` entries (slot=`face`)?

import os, json
from sqlalchemy import text
from models import SessionLocal

db = SessionLocal()
try:
    photo_refs = {fn for (fn,) in db.execute(text("SELECT photo_filename FROM user_photos WHERE photo_filename IS NOT NULL")).all()}
    voice_refs = {fn for (fn,) in db.execute(text("SELECT voice_note FROM likes WHERE voice_note IS NOT NULL")).all()}
    media_refs = {fn for (fn,) in db.execute(text("SELECT filename FROM user_media")).all()}
finally:
    db.close()

def audit(dirname, refs):
    files = os.listdir(dirname)
    total_bytes = sum(os.path.getsize(os.path.join(dirname, f)) for f in files)
    orphans = [f for f in files if f not in refs]
    orphan_bytes = sum(os.path.getsize(os.path.join(dirname, f)) for f in orphans)
    return files, total_bytes, orphans, orphan_bytes

for name, refs in [("photos", photo_refs), ("voice_notes", voice_refs), ("media", media_refs)]:
    files, tb, orph, ob = audit(name, refs)
    print(f"[{name}] files={len(files)} total={tb/1024:.1f} KiB | orphans={len(orph)} orphan_bytes={ob/1024:.1f} KiB")
    with open(f"/tmp/m88/orphans_{name}.txt", "w") as f:
        for o in sorted(orph):
            p = os.path.join(name, o)
            f.write(f"{os.path.getsize(p):>9d}  {p}\n")
PY

# ── spec generation (deterministic) ─────────────────────────────────────────

def pick_photos(n):
    """Take the n highest-numbered photoface_*.jpg files that exist locally.
    Skips gaps (e.g. missing 301, 402, 403). Raises if fewer than n exist."""
    files = glob.glob(os.path.join(LOCAL_PHOTO_DIR, "photoface_*.jpg"))
    def num(p):
        m = re.search(r"photoface_(\d+)\.jpg", p)
        return int(m.group(1)) if m else -1
    files = [p for p in files if num(p) > 0]
    files.sort(key=num, reverse=True)
    if len(files) < n:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Need {n} photos, only {len(files)} available in {LOCAL_PHOTO_DIR}")
    return [os.path.basename(p) for p in files[:n]]


def gen_usernames(n, rng):
    seen, out = set(), []
    while len(out) < n:
        u = f"{rng.choice(ADJECTIVES)}{rng.choice(NOUNS)}{rng.randint(10, 999)}"
        if "jason" in u.lower() or u in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(u)
        out.append(u)
    return out


def height_for_sex(sex, rng):
    if sex in ("Male", "Transgender Male"):
        return rng.randint(165, 195)
    if sex in ("Female", "Transgender Female"):
        return rng.randint(152, 183)
    return rng.randint(155, 190)  # Not Specified

content.text:
---

[M88 — Phase 1 DRY-RUN report]

**Read-only audit complete. Found one material issue that changes the plan before any quarantine happens — flagging for your call.**

### Orphan inventory (with sizes)

| Bucket | Count | Total size | Files list |
|---|---|---|---|
| `photos/` orphans | **390** | **2,424.1 KiB (~2.4 MiB)** | `/tmp/m88/orphans_photos.txt` |
| `voice_notes/` orphans | **123** | **1,925.8 KiB (~1.9 MiB)** | `/tmp/m88/orphans_voice_notes.txt` |
| `media/` orphans | **1** | **6.1 KiB** | `/tmp/m88/orphans_media.txt` (just `media/gallery_32_face.jpg`) |
| **Combined** | 514 | **~4.35 MiB** | |

### Phase 1.4 finding — seed scripts DO read from the orphans

**Photos orphans are the reseed pool. Quarantining them breaks future seeding.**

- **`seed_30_extra_users.py`**: hard-codes 30 specific filenames (`photoface_12.jpg`, `_25`, `_33`, `_41`, `_56`, …, `_226`, …). **All 30 are in the orphan list** — quarantining takes this script from "works" to "FileNotFoundError on the first user".
- **`seed_200_extra_users.py`** (`pick_photos`, line 178): `glob.glob(photos/photoface_*.jpg)` → takes the `n` highest-numbered. Raises `RuntimeError` if pool < n. With only 21 files left (the ones in `user_photos`), seeding >21 new users fails instantly.
- **`reseed_20_users.py`** (line 235-242): `os.listdir(photos/)` + numeric descending sort → same pool pattern. Fails when `N_USERS` > remaining pool.
- **`seed_all_users.py`**: `PHOTOS_DIR = "photos"` + reads it.

**Voice orphans are ALL stale `like_*.m4a` (from deleted/rerolled likes).** Safe to quarantine:
- 0 of 123 are `sample_*.m4a` pool files — the `sample_*.m4a` pool actually lives in a separate root-level directory `sample_voice_notes/` (`sample1.m4a`, `sample2.m4a`, …), not `voice_notes/`.
- All 123 orphans follow `voice_notes/like_<liker>_<liked>.m4a` — no seed script reads by listdir from here, and references to them in DB are already gone.

**Media orphan (1 file) is safe**: `media/gallery_32_face.jpg` points at user_id=32 which doesn't exist (max = 26).

### Revised Phase 2 recommendation

**Do NOT quarantine `photos/` orphans as originally planned.** Three alternatives:

- **(A) Quarantine voice + media only; leave photos pool as-is.** ~1.93 MiB freed. Keeps all seed scripts working out of the box. My default recommendation.
- **(B) Still quarantine photos orphans + retire the dependent seed scripts.** Frees +2.4 MiB but deletes 4 working seed scripts (you'd lose `reseed_20_users.py`, `seed_30_extra_users.py`, etc.). Worth it only if you've decided those scripts are themselves obsolete under the slot-refactor.
- **(C) Rename `photos/` → `photos_pool/` (source of truth), and create a new `photos/` that contains only the 22 live user_photos files. Update seed scripts to read from `photos_pool/`.** Clean separation, but 1-line edit across ~4 scripts. Good if you want "live vs pool" clearly delineated going forward.

### Disk space summary

- **Option A** (voice + media only): **~1.93 MiB** freed, 124 files quarantined, 0 seed scripts broken.
- **Option B** (full quarantine + script purge): **~4.35 MiB** freed, 514 files quarantined, 4+ seed scripts deleted/archived.
- **Option C** (photos_pool rename): **0 MiB** freed immediately but clean structure; we can then safely quarantine stale voice+media as in A.

### Phase 3–5 status

Independent of the photos-orphan decision:
- Phase 3 (backfill 22 legacy `user_photos` → `user_media` slot=`face`) can proceed as-is.
- Phase 4 (audit code paths that read `user_photos` directly + deprecate comment in `models.py`) can proceed.
- Phase 5 (commit + push `fix/110-backend-consolidated`) same.

---

**Pausing here for your decision. Which option for photos (A/B/C)?** Once you pick, I'll run Phase 2 quarantine → Phase 3 backfill → Phase 4 audit → Phase 5 commit/push as one sequence, and include backend restart/verify commands per M82 when I need you to re-check login.

[M88 — Phase 1 complete, awaiting approval for Phase 2]

message.content:
[M91] Backend restructure for new gender + sexuality + position scheme.

Goal: enable new spec for sex (5 options), sexuality (2 options), and position (5 options conditionally).

EXECUTE IN ORDER. AUTO-APPROVE local. Pause before any prod deploy.

PHASE 1 — Schema additions
1. Add position column to User model (models.py:13-40):
   position = Column(String, nullable=True)
   Allowed values: 'Top','Both more top','Both','Both more bottom','Bottom', NULL

2. Update sex column allowed values (currently Male/Female/Transgender Female/Transgender Male/Not Specified):
   New scheme:
   - Male
   - Female
   - Ladyboy/Trans❓
   - Ladyboy/Trans🍆
   - Ladyboy/Trans🦪

3. Update sexuality column allowed values (currently Straight/Gay/Bisexual/Pansexual/Not Specified):
   New scheme:
   - Straight
   - Other

PHASE 2 — Migration
4. Create migrate_gender_sexuality_position.py:
   - Add position column to users table (nullable)
   - Migrate existing sex values:
     * 'Transgender Female' → 'Ladyboy/Trans❓'
     * 'Transgender Male' → 'Ladyboy/Trans❓' (or stay Male if appropriate — discuss)
     * 'Not Specified' → 'Not Specified' (keep) or migrate to Male as fallback (discuss)
   - Migrate existing sexuality values:
     * 'Straight' → 'Straight' (no change)
     * 'Gay', 'Bisexual', 'Pansexual', 'Not Specified' → 'Other'
   - Idempotent (skip if already migrated)
5. Run migration on local DevDB
6. Verify counts: SELECT sex, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY sex; SELECT sexuality, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY sexuality;

PHASE 3 — Schema validation
7. Update Pydantic schemas in schemas.py:
   - RegisterRequest: sex/sexuality use new enums, position field added (Optional[str])
   - PUT /users/me schema: same additions
   - ProfileResponse

content.text:
Pausing before any file edits — the prompt looks truncated and has two explicit "(discuss)" markers I need your call on. Also [M88] is still mid-flight.

### Open items to resolve before I execute [M91]

1. **Prompt appears cut off.** It ends at "— ProfileResponse" with no phases 4/5, no closing bracket, no `Reply [M91]` line. Want to confirm there isn't a Phase 4 (routes.py validator enum updates, test seed-user values, FE coordination, etc.) and a Phase 5 (commit/deploy) you meant to send.

2. **Step 4 — Transgender Male migration.** Two reasonable options:
   - (a) `Transgender Male` → `Ladyboy/Trans❓` (treat all trans values identically; consistent with the "Transgender Female" → `Ladyboy/Trans❓` rule)
   - (b) `Transgender Male` → `Male` (respects self-ID on the masculine axis, but collapses gender identity info)
   Recommend **(a)** — it preserves the trans signal, matches what you did with Transgender Female, and the new scheme has `Ladyboy/Trans❓` as the "unspecified sub-type" flavour. Yes?

3. **Step 4 — `Not Specified` sex migration.** Options:
   - (a) Keep `Not Specified` as a legal value (adds a 6th to the enum — breaks the "5 options" goal)
   - (b) Migrate to `Male` as fallback (loses info, biases the data)
   - (c) Migrate to `Ladyboy/Trans❓` (treat "unknown" as "trans unspecified")
   - (d) Set to `NULL` and make the column nullable (preserves "unknown", no schema pollution)
   Recommend **(d)** — matches the nullable pattern already used for `age`, `sex`, `sexuality` etc. in models.py:22-23. Keeps the enum at 5 and represents "unknown" honestly.

4. **Fix-branch.** We're currently on `fix/110-backend-consolidated` from prior work. This schema change is a separate initiative. Per the fix-branch workflow memory, should I:
   - (a) Merge/finish `fix/110-backend-consolidated` first, then cut `fix/111-gender-sexuality-position` off development
   - (b) Stack `fix/111-...` on top of `fix/110-...` (messier — M110 would block M111 review)
   - (c) Just do it on `fix/110-backend-consolidated` as one mega-PR (violates the one-fix-per-branch spirit)
   Recommend **(a)**. Quick call: is `fix/110-backend-consolidated` done and ready to merge to `development`, or still in-progress?

5. **[M88] still paused.** Phase 1 dry-run ended with a recommendation for option **A** (quarantine voice+media only, leave the `photos/` reseed pool alone), awaiting your A/B/C pick. Two choices here:
   - (a) Abandon [M88] — drop it, move on to [M91]
   - (b) Quickly finish [M88] option A first (a few minutes: quarantine 124 files, backfill 22 `user_media` rows, commit, push), *then* start [M91] on a fresh branch
   Recommend **(b)** so we don't leave a half-done audit sitting; the backfill is also cheap and uncontroversial.

### Minor notes (not blockers)

- The new sex values include three emoji-bearing strings (`❓`, `🍆`, `🦪`). Storable in SQLite `TEXT`/Python `str` with no issue; FE will need to render them correctly (display + probably used as enum keys on form submission). Flagging because CLAUDE.md has a general "no emojis unless the user asks" rule and here you're explicitly asking — fine, just want to confirm these literal strings (including the exact emoji codepoints) are the intended stored values, not display-only labels for an underlying numeric/string enum like `trans_unspecified` / `trans_m` / `trans_f`. **Storing display labels directly as DB enum values makes future renames painful**; recommend using a stable internal key (e.g. `trans_u`, `trans_penis`, `trans_vagina`) with the emoji-labels applied at the UI layer.
- `schemas.py` in this repo is currently tiny (203 bytes). Most request/response models live in `routes.py` imports or are inline — I'll need to survey the real validator locations (models.py, routes.py validators, schemas.py) before step 7.
- Step 2 `position` column: nullable is correct — it's conditional on sex per the M91 header ("position (5 options conditionally)"). The *conditionality* (e.g. "only applies when sex ∈ {Ladyboy/Trans🍆}" or similar) isn't specified — need the rule for when position must/may be set so the validator rejects or allows it appropriately.

Send me (a) the resolutions to 2/3/4/5 and (b) the missing Phase 4/5 and the position-conditionality rule, and I'll execute in one pass with commit + push on the appropriate fix branch.

message.content:
[M91 cont] Resolutions:

Q2: (a) Transgender Male → trans_unspecified
Q3: (d) Not Specified → NULL, sex column nullable
Q4: (a) Finish M88 first then cut fix/111 off development
Q5: M88 = Option C (NOT A) — photos_pool rename + new photos/ with 22 live files + update 4 seed scripts

DESIGN UPDATE — store stable internal keys, not emoji literals:
- sex enum: male, female, trans_unspecified, trans_eggplant, trans_clam (plus NULL)
- sexuality enum: straight, other
- position enum: top, both_more_top, both, both_more_bottom, bottom (plus NULL)
- Frontend renders display labels + emojis from these keys

POSITION CONDITIONALITY: backend just stores. Position column nullable. Allowed list validation only. Frontend handles conditional show.

EXECUTION ORDER:
1. Finish M88 Option C (photos_pool rename + slot backfill + commit + push fix/110-backend-consolidated)
2. AFTER M88 commits + pushes, then start M91 on NEW branch fix/111-gender-sexuality-position off development tip
3. M91 Phases as drafted (schema, migration, validation, endpoints, test, commit + push)

Reply [M91-cont] when M88 done, then proceed with M91.

def pick_photos(n):
    files = [
        f for f in os.listdir(PHOTOS_DIR)
        if re.match(r"photoface_\d+\.jpg$", f)
    ]
    if len(files) < n:
        raise RuntimeError(f"need {n} photos, have {len(files)} in {PHOTOS_DIR}")
    # Sort numerically descending (use highest-numbered photos to avoid
    # whatever the first 30 seeded scripts historically reserved).
    files.sort(key=lambda f: int(re.search(r"(\d+)", f).group(1)), reverse=True)
    return files[:n]


def height_for_sex(sex, rng):
    if sex in ("Male", "Transgender Male"):
        return rng.randint(165, 200)
    if sex in ("Female", "Transgender Female"):
        return rng.randint(155, 185)
    return rng.randint(158, 195)  # Not Specified


def build_specs(rng):
    usernames = gen_usernames(N_USERS, rng)
    photos = pick_photos(N_USERS)
    genders = list(GENDER_PLAN)
    rng.shuffle(genders)
    bios = rng.sample(BIOS, N_USERS)
    specs = []
    for username, photo, sex, bio in zip(usernames, photos, genders, bios):
        specs.append({
            "username":  username,
            "age":       int(rng.triangular(21, 55, 32)),
            "sex":       sex,
            "sexuality": rng.choice(SEXUALITIES),
            "star_sign": rng.choice(STAR_SIGNS),
            "height":    height_for_sex(sex, rng),
            "bio":       bio,
            "photo":     photo,
        })
    return specs


# ── direct SQL for profile fields ───────────────────────────────────────────

def apply_profile_fields(specs, id_by_username):
    """Update age/sex/sexuality/star_sign/height/bio + insert user_photos."""
    def sqlesc(s):
        return "'" + s.replace("'", "''") + "'"
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    c = conn.cursor()
    for spec in specs:
        uid = id_by_username[spec["username"]]
        c.execute(
            "UPDATE users SET age=?, sex=?, sexuality=?, star_sign=?, height=?, bio=? WHERE id=?",
            (spec["age"], spec["sex"], spec["sexuality"], spec["star_sign"],
             spec["height"], spec["bio"], uid),
        )
        c.execute(
            "INSERT INTO user_photos (user_id, photo_filename) VALUES (?, ?)",
            (uid, spec["photo"]),
        )
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()


# ── main ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    print(f"=== STEP 1: stop backend ===")
    stop_backend()
    print()

    print(f"=== STEP 2: wipe DB + re-init schema ===")
    wipe_db()
    print(f"  {DB_PATH} recreated (empty schema)")

input.command:
cd /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20
DATE=20260424
BACKUP_DIR=".orphans_backup_${DATE}"

echo "=== 1. create photos_pool/ (full copy of photos/) ==="
cp -a photos photos_pool
echo "photos_pool/: $(ls photos_pool | wc -l) files"

echo
echo "=== 2. derive the 22 live user-id filenames ==="
./venv/bin/python3 - <<'PY' > /tmp/m88/live_keep.txt
from sqlalchemy import text
from models import SessionLocal
db = SessionLocal()
try:
    uids = [r[0] for r in db.execute(text("SELECT user_id FROM user_photos WHERE photo_filename IS NOT NULL")).all()]
    for uid in sorted(set(uids)):
        print(f"photoface_{uid}.jpg")
finally:
    db.close()
PY
wc -l /tmp/m88/live_keep.txt
echo '--- live set ---'
cat /tmp/m88/live_keep.txt

echo
echo "=== 3. remove non-live files from photos/ (pool has the copies) ==="
cd photos
removed=0
for f in *; do
    if ! grep -qx "$f" /tmp/m88/live_keep.txt; then
        rm -- "$f"
        removed=$((removed+1))
    fi
done
cd ..
echo "photos/ now: $(ls photos | wc -l) files (expect 22)"
echo "removed: $removed (expect 389)"

echo
echo "=== 4. create orphan backup dir + quarantine 123 voice + 1 media ==="
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR/voice_notes" "$BACKUP_DIR/media"
# voice: move each path listed in orphans_voice_notes.txt (format: 'SIZE  voice_notes/FILE')
while read -r _ path; do
    [ -z "$path" ] && continue
    mv "$path" "$BACKUP_DIR/voice_notes/" 2>/dev/null || echo "MISS: $path"
done < /tmp/m88/orphans_voice_notes.txt
echo "voice quarantined: $(ls $BACKUP_DIR/voice_notes | wc -l) (expect 123)"

mv media/gallery_32_face.jpg "$BACKUP_DIR/media/" 2>/dev/null && echo "media quarantined: gallery_32_face.jpg"

echo
echo "=== 5. final disk state ==="
echo "photos/        : $(ls photos | wc -l)"
echo "photos_pool/   : $(ls photos_pool | wc -l)"
echo "voice_notes/   : $(ls voice_notes | wc -l)"
echo "media/         : $(ls media | wc -l)"
echo "$BACKUP_DIR/   : $(find $BACKUP_DIR -type f | wc -l)"

import os, shutil
from sqlalchemy import text
from models import SessionLocal

BACKUP = ".orphans_backup_20260424"
os.makedirs(f"{BACKUP}/voice_notes", exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs(f"{BACKUP}/media", exist_ok=True)

db = SessionLocal()
try:
    # voice orphans
    refs = {r[0] for r in db.execute(text("SELECT voice_note FROM likes WHERE voice_note IS NOT NULL")).all()}
    voice_files = os.listdir("voice_notes")
    moved_v = 0
    for f in voice_files:
        if f not in refs:
            shutil.move(os.path.join("voice_notes", f), os.path.join(BACKUP, "voice_notes", f))
            moved_v += 1
    print(f"voice orphans moved: {moved_v}")
    print(f"voice_notes/ remaining: {len(os.listdir('voice_notes'))}")

    # media orphans
    refs = {r[0] for r in db.execute(text("SELECT filename FROM user_media")).all()}
    moved_m = 0
    for f in os.listdir("media"):
        p = os.path.join("media", f)
        if os.path.isfile(p) and f not in refs:
            shutil.move(p, os.path.join(BACKUP, "media", f))
            moved_m += 1
    print(f"media orphans moved: {moved_m}")
    print(f"media/ remaining: {sorted(os.listdir('media'))}")
finally:
    db.close()

from models import SessionLocal, UserMedia, UserPhoto
db = SessionLocal()
try:
    before = db.query(UserMedia).count()
    photos = db.query(UserPhoto).filter(UserPhoto.photo_filename.isnot(None)).all()
    inserted, skipped = 0, 0
    for p in photos:
        exists = db.query(UserMedia).filter_by(user_id=p.user_id, slot='face').first()
        if exists:
            skipped += 1
            continue
        db.add(UserMedia(user_id=p.user_id, slot='face', filename=p.photo_filename))
        inserted += 1
    db.commit()
    after = db.query(UserMedia).count()
    print(f"user_media before: {before}")
    print(f"user_photos with filename: {len(photos)}")
    print(f"inserted: {inserted}")
    print(f"skipped (already had face slot): {skipped}")
    print(f"user_media after: {after}  (expected {before + inserted})")
finally:
    db.close()
