class RankOneLike(Base):
    __tablename__ = "rank_one_likes"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    timer_started_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    # M104: two-phase rank-1. expires_at is now populated at the activation
    # tick (when grace ends and lock starts), so it's nullable on creation.
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    status = Column(String, default="pending", nullable=False)  # pending, matched, like_expired
    # M104: grace→locked transition markers.
    #   lock_activated_at IS NULL  -> row is in GRACE phase (user can still
    #                                 unlike; slide-left allowed on FE).

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
M104: add two-phase grace→locked columns to rank_one_likes.

Columns:
  - lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP NULL   (NULL while in grace)
  - activates_at      TIMESTAMP NULL   (when grace ends → lock starts)

Also: expires_at becomes semantically nullable (previously always set at
creation). SQLite doesn't enforce nullability for existing columns, so the
models.py change is enough there.

Existing pending rows are backfilled so they appear as ALREADY-LOCKED (the
safe choice: they've been sitting around waiting to expire, not sitting in
a grace window). For each pending row:
  lock_activated_at = timer_started_at
  activates_at      = timer_started_at  (grace "ended" in the past)
  expires_at        stays as-is

Idempotent: runs a no-op if both columns already exist.

Usage:
    ./venv/bin/python3 migrate_rank_one_grace.py [db_path]
"""
import sqlite3
import sys


def column_exists(cur, table, col):
    return col in {row[1] for row in cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall()}


def migrate(db_path: str):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cur = conn.cursor()

    added = []
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "lock_activated_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("lock_activated_at")
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "activates_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN activates_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("activates_at")

    if added:
        print(f"[migrate] added columns: {added}")
    else:
        print("[migrate] lock_activated_at + activates_at already present — no column change")

    # Backfill — only touches rows where lock_activated_at IS NULL (fresh on
    # first run; nothing matches on subsequent runs). Treat existing pending
    # rows as "already locked" so the scheduler's expiry tick behaves as
    # before for them.
    n = cur.execute(
        """
        UPDATE rank_one_likes
        SET lock_activated_at = timer_started_at,
            activates_at      = timer_started_at
        WHERE lock_activated_at IS NULL
          AND status = 'pending'
        """
    ).rowcount
    print(f"[migrate] backfilled {n} pending row(s) as already-locked")

    conn.commit()

    # Post-state
    print("\n[migrate] post-state — rank_one_likes phase breakdown:")
    for row in cur.execute(
        """
        SELECT status,
               CASE
                 WHEN lock_activated_at IS NULL THEN 'grace'
                 ELSE 'locked'
               END AS phase,
               COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM rank_one_likes
        GROUP BY status, phase
        """
    ):
        print(f"  status={row[0]!r:>15}  phase={row[1]!r}  n={row[2]}")

    conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    db_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "DevDB.sqlite"
    migrate(db_path)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
from datetime import datetime
from sqlalchemy import Boolean, Column, DateTime, Integer, String, Text, Date, create_engine, ForeignKey, UniqueConstraint
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from config import DATABASE_URL

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False})
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

Base = declarative_base()


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    username = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    hashed_password = Column(String)
    date_of_birth = Column(Date, nullable=True)
    age = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    # M91 (2026-04-24): stable internal keys, FE renders display labels+emojis.
    sex = Column(String, nullable=True)  # male/female/trans_unspecified/trans_eggplant/trans_clam, NULL allowed
    sexuality = Column(String, nullable=True)  # straight/other, NULL allowed
    bio = Column(String, nullable=True)
    headline = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    height = Column(Integer, nullable=True)  # cm
    star_sign = Column(String, nullable=True)
    dating_speed = Column(String, default='1day')  # '1min', '1hour', '1day', '1week'
    timezone = Column(String, default='Europe/London', nullable=True)
    last_lock_activation = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    referred_by = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=True, index=True)
    # M91: position preference. Conditional on sex at the FE; backend only validates allowed values.
    position = Column(String, nullable=True)  # top/both_more_top/both/both_more_bottom/bottom, NULL allowed

    photo = relationship("UserPhoto", back_populates="user", uselist=False)


# DEPRECATED (M88): superseded by UserMedia(slot='face'). Retained for back-compat
# reads in routes.py (/users/me, /users/{id}, browse, upload). Do not add new
# callers. Plan to migrate readers and drop this table in a future fix.
class UserPhoto(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_photos"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), unique=True, nullable=False)
    photo_filename = Column(String, nullable=True)  # stored filename e.g. "photoface_<user_id>.jpg"
    photo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)        # original source URL

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="photo")


class Like(Base):
    __tablename__ = "likes"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("liker_id", "liked_id", name="uq_like_pair"),)
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    voice_note = Column(String, nullable=True)  # filename e.g. "like_123_456.m4a"


class Block(Base):
    __tablename__ = "blocks"
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    blocker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    blocked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    # nullable so the prod migration succeeds without backfill; new rows
    # get stamped via the python-side default on insert.
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=True)


class UserLikeStats(Base):
    """Personal like ranking — one row per (liker, liked) pair.

    username  : the user doing the liking
    user_likes: the user being liked
    like_rank : 1-based position in THIS user's personal liked list (1 = top)
    """
    __tablename__ = "senders_like_ranking"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("username", "user_likes", name="uq_senders_like_ranking_pair"),)

    id         = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username   = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    user_likes = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    like_rank  = Column(Integer, nullable=True)


class Match(Base):
    __tablename__ = "matches"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user1_id", "user2_id", name="uq_match_pair"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True, autoincrement=True)
    user1_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    user2_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Message(Base):
    __tablename__ = "messages"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    sender_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    receiver_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    content = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)
    is_read = Column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)


class RankOneLike(Base):
    __tablename__ = "rank_one_likes"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    timer_started_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="pending", nullable=False)  # pending, matched, like_expired


class ReferrerEvent(Base):
    __tablename__ = "referrer_events"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    event_name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    value = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=0)
    referrer_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    referred_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class UserMedia(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_media"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user_id", "slot", name="uq_user_media_slot"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    slot = Column(String, nullable=False)  # face, full_length, happy, funny, hobby, video
    filename = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Feedback(Base):
    __tablename__ = "feedback"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    # nullable so an anonymous-feedback flow can be added later without
    # another migration; for now the POST endpoint requires auth.
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True, index=True)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    screenshot_path = Column(String, nullable=True)
    app_version = Column(String, nullable=False)
    platform = Column(String, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="new", nullable=False)  # new | read | resolved
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False, index=True)


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

def expire_rank_one_likes_1week() -> None:
    process_rank_one_expiry('1week')


# ── M104: activation tick ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Runs frequently (every 10s in dev, every minute in prod). For each pending
# row whose grace window has ended (activates_at <= now) but that hasn't
# been locked yet (lock_activated_at IS NULL), flip it to the locked phase:
# set lock_activated_at = now, compute expires_at = now + lock_duration for
# the liker's dating_speed. The existing per-speed expire jobs then pick up
# the row at its expires_at.
def tick_activation() -> None:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        now = datetime.utcnow()
        pending = (
            db.query(RankOneLike)
            .filter(
                RankOneLike.status == "pending",
                RankOneLike.lock_activated_at.is_(None),
                RankOneLike.activates_at <= now,
            )
            .all()
        )
        if not pending:
            return
        activated = 0
        for lock in pending:
            liker = db.query(User).filter(User.id == lock.liker_id).first()
            speed = (liker.dating_speed if liker else None) or "1day"
            lock.lock_activated_at = now
            lock.expires_at = now + _timer_duration_for_speed(speed)
            activated += 1
            print(
                f"[tick_activation] lock id={lock.id} liker={lock.liker_id} "
                f"-> LOCKED until {lock.expires_at} (speed={speed})"
            )
        db.commit()
        # Nudge affected likers' WIL so the FE phase label flips live.
        try:
            from routes import bump_wil_version
            for lk in pending:
                bump_wil_version(lk.liker_id)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[tick_activation] bump_wil_version failed: {e}")
        print(f"[tick_activation] activated {activated} row(s) at {now}")
    except Exception as e:
        db.rollback()
        print(f"[tick_activation] error: {e}")
    finally:
        db.close()

# Idempotent create job — fires every minute on the :00 boundary.
    scheduler.add_job(
        create_rank_one_likes,
        CronTrigger(second=0),
        id="create_rank_one_likes",
        replace_existing=True,
    )

    # TZ-aware tick for 1day / 1week speeds — runs every minute on :00.
    # Replaces the per-tier 1day/1week cron jobs. Required to honor each
    # user's local-TZ 21:00 trigger instead of a fixed UTC midnight.
    scheduler.add_job(
        tick_tz_aware_activations,
        CronTrigger(second=0),
        id="tick_tz_aware_activations",
        replace_existing=True,
    )
    print(f"[scheduler] Registered tz-aware tick: every minute on :00")

    # Login rate-limit alert tick (M44). Window=60s by default; tick runs
    # at the same cadence so each pass covers one window of nginx 429 log
    # activity. Reads /var/log/nginx/login_rate_limit.log since bookmark
    # and emails admins if any IP crossed the threshold.
    rl_window = config.config.integrations.login_rate_limit_alert_window_seconds
    scheduler.add_job(
        tick_login_rate_limit_alerts,
        'interval',
        seconds=rl_window,
        id="tick_login_rate_limit_alerts",
        replace_existing=True,
    )
    print(f"[scheduler] Registered login rate-limit alert tick: every {rl_window}s")

    # In DEV_MODE, run expiry every 10 seconds
    if DEV_MODE:
        print(f"[scheduler] DEV_MODE enabled — registering 10-second expiry job")
        scheduler.add_job(
            expire_rank_one_likes_dev,
            'interval',
            seconds=10,
            id="expire_rank_one_likes_dev",
            replace_existing=True,
        )
        print(f"[scheduler] Registered DEV expire job: every 10 seconds")
    else:
        # Production: register expire jobs for 1min/1hour only.
        # 1day and 1week are handled by tick_tz_aware_activations above.
        tier_functions = {
            '1min': expire_rank_one_likes_1min,
            '1hour': expire_rank_one_likes_1hour,
        }

        for speed in get_all_speeds():
            if speed not in tier_functions:
                # 1day / 1week skipped — tz-aware tick covers them
                continue

            cron_schedule = get_cron_schedule(speed)
            trigger = _cron_trigger_from_string(cron_schedule)

            scheduler.add_job(
                tier_functions[speed],
                trigger,
                id=f"expire_rank_one_likes_{speed}",
                replace_existing=True,
            )
            print(f"[scheduler] Registered expire job for {speed}: {cron_schedule}")

    scheduler.start()

    jobs = [j.id for j in scheduler.get_jobs()]
    print(f"[scheduler] Started with jobs: {jobs}")
    return scheduler

# M104: include both GRACE (lock_activated_at IS NULL) and LOCKED rows
    # that aren't yet expired. A row is "active" iff it's pending AND
    # (still in grace OR locked with time remaining).
    from sqlalchemy import or_
    _active = lambda q: q.filter(
        RankOneLike.status == "pending",
        or_(
            RankOneLike.lock_activated_at.is_(None),
            RankOneLike.expires_at > now,
        ),
    )

    as_liker_rank_entry = _active(
        db.query(RankOneLike).filter(RankOneLike.liker_id == current_user.id)
    ).first()

    as_liked_rank_entry = _active(
        db.query(RankOneLike).filter(RankOneLike.liked_id == current_user.id)
    ).first()

    def _build_info(entry, other_user):
        return RankOneLikeInfo(
            rank_one_id=entry.id,
            timer_started_at=entry.timer_started_at.isoformat() + "Z",

            activates_at=_iso_or_none(entry.activates_at),
            lock_activated_at=_iso_or_none(entry.lock_activated_at),
            phase=_rank_one_phase(entry),
            status=entry.status,
            user=_rank_one_user_info(other_user),
        )

    as_liker = None
    if as_liker_rank_entry:
        liked_user = db.query(User).filter(User.id == as_liker_rank_entry.liked_id).first()

@router.delete("/like/{liked_user_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def unlike_user(
    liked_user_id: int,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Remove a like and resequence the personal like_rank for remaining likes.

    M104: if a RankOneLike row exists for this pair AND it's still in the
    grace phase (lock_activated_at IS NULL), delete it too. Locked rows
    block unlike on the FE via rank_one_phase='locked', so we shouldn't
    see a locked-row delete reach here — but if one does, raise 409 so
    the FE surfaces the violation rather than silently bypassing the lock.

@router.delete("/like/{liked_user_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def unlike_user(
    liked_user_id: int,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Remove a like and resequence the personal like_rank for remaining likes.

    M104: if a RankOneLike row exists for this pair AND it's still in the
    grace phase (lock_activated_at IS NULL), delete it too. Locked rows
    block unlike on the FE via rank_one_phase='locked', so we shouldn't
    see a locked-row delete reach here — but if one does, raise 409 so
    the FE surfaces the violation rather than silently bypassing the lock.
    """
    liked_user = db.query(User).filter(User.id == liked_user_id).first()

    # M104: guard against unliking a LOCKED rank-1 row
    existing_lock = db.query(RankOneLike).filter(
        RankOneLike.liker_id == current_user.id,
        RankOneLike.liked_id == liked_user_id,
        RankOneLike.status == "pending",
    ).first()
    if existing_lock is not None and existing_lock.lock_activated_at is not None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
            detail="Cannot unlike: rank-1 lock is active",
        )
    if existing_lock is not None:
        # grace-phase row — safe to delete alongside the Like/stat
        db.delete(existing_lock)

    # Remove Like row
    like = db.query(Like).filter(
        Like.liker_id == current_user.id,
        Like.liked_id == liked_user_id,
    ).first()
    if like:
        db.delete(like)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
M104: add two-phase grace→locked columns to rank_one_likes.

Columns:
  - lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP NULL   (NULL while in grace)
  - activates_at      TIMESTAMP NULL   (when grace ends → lock starts)

Also: expires_at becomes semantically nullable (previously always set at
creation). SQLite doesn't enforce nullability for existing columns, so the
models.py change is enough there.

Existing pending rows are backfilled so they appear as ALREADY-LOCKED (the
safe choice: they've been sitting around waiting to expire, not sitting in
a grace window). For each pending row:
  lock_activated_at = timer_started_at
  activates_at      = timer_started_at  (grace "ended" in the past)
  expires_at        stays as-is

Idempotent: runs a no-op if both columns already exist.

Usage:
    ./venv/bin/python3 migrate_rank_one_grace.py [db_path]
"""
import sqlite3
import sys


def column_exists(cur, table, col):
    return col in {row[1] for row in cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall()}


def migrate(db_path: str):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cur = conn.cursor()

    added = []
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "lock_activated_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("lock_activated_at")
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "activates_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN activates_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("activates_at")

    if added:
        print(f"[migrate] added columns: {added}")
    else:
        print("[migrate] lock_activated_at + activates_at already present — no column change")

    # Backfill — only touches rows where lock_activated_at IS NULL (fresh on
    # first run; nothing matches on subsequent runs). Treat existing pending
    # rows as "already locked" so the scheduler's expiry tick behaves as
    # before for them.
    n = cur.execute(
        """
        UPDATE rank_one_likes
        SET lock_activated_at = timer_started_at,
            activates_at      = timer_started_at
        WHERE lock_activated_at IS NULL
          AND status = 'pending'
        """
    ).rowcount
    print(f"[migrate] backfilled {n} pending row(s) as already-locked")

    conn.commit()

    # Post-state
    print("\n[migrate] post-state — rank_one_likes phase breakdown:")
    for row in cur.execute(
        """
        SELECT status,
               CASE
                 WHEN lock_activated_at IS NULL THEN 'grace'
                 ELSE 'locked'
               END AS phase,
               COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM rank_one_likes
        GROUP BY status, phase
        """
    ):
        print(f"  status={row[0]!r:>15}  phase={row[1]!r}  n={row[2]}")

    conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    db_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "DevDB.sqlite"
    migrate(db_path)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
M104: add two-phase grace→locked columns to rank_one_likes.

Columns:
  - lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP NULL   (NULL while in grace)
  - activates_at      TIMESTAMP NULL   (when grace ends → lock starts)

Also: expires_at becomes semantically nullable (previously always set at
creation). SQLite doesn't enforce nullability for existing columns, so the
models.py change is enough there.

Existing pending rows are backfilled so they appear as ALREADY-LOCKED (the
safe choice: they've been sitting around waiting to expire, not sitting in
a grace window). For each pending row:
  lock_activated_at = timer_started_at
  activates_at      = timer_started_at  (grace "ended" in the past)
  expires_at        stays as-is

Idempotent: runs a no-op if both columns already exist.

Usage:
    ./venv/bin/python3 migrate_rank_one_grace.py [db_path]
"""
import sqlite3
import sys


def column_exists(cur, table, col):
    return col in {row[1] for row in cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall()}


def column_not_null(cur, table, col):
    """Return True if the column is declared NOT NULL in the DB schema."""
    for row in cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall():
        if row[1] == col:
            return bool(row[3])  # notnull field
    return False


def make_expires_at_nullable(cur):
    """SQLite can't ALTER COLUMN directly — recreate the table if expires_at
    is still NOT NULL. No-op once the column is already nullable."""
    if not column_not_null(cur, "rank_one_likes", "expires_at"):
        print("[migrate] rank_one_likes.expires_at already nullable — skipping table rebuild")
        return
    print("[migrate] rebuilding rank_one_likes to make expires_at nullable")
    cur.executescript(
        """
        CREATE TABLE rank_one_likes_new (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            liker_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
            liked_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
            timer_started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
            expires_at TIMESTAMP,
            status VARCHAR NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
            lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP,
            activates_at TIMESTAMP
        );
        INSERT INTO rank_one_likes_new
            (id, liker_id, liked_id, timer_started_at, expires_at,
             status, lock_activated_at, activates_at)
        SELECT id, liker_id, liked_id, timer_started_at, expires_at,
               status, lock_activated_at, activates_at
        FROM rank_one_likes;
        DROP TABLE rank_one_likes;
        ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes_new RENAME TO rank_one_likes;
        CREATE INDEX ix_rank_one_likes_id ON rank_one_likes(id);
        """
    )


def migrate(db_path: str):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cur = conn.cursor()

    added = []
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "lock_activated_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("lock_activated_at")
    if not column_exists(cur, "rank_one_likes", "activates_at"):
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE rank_one_likes ADD COLUMN activates_at TIMESTAMP")
        added.append("activates_at")

    if added:
        print(f"[migrate] added columns: {added}")
    else:
        print("[migrate] lock_activated_at + activates_at already present — no column change")

    # Backfill — only touches rows where lock_activated_at IS NULL (fresh on
    # first run; nothing matches on subsequent runs). Treat existing pending
    # rows as "already locked" so the scheduler's expiry tick behaves as
    # before for them.
    n = cur.execute(
        """
        UPDATE rank_one_likes
        SET lock_activated_at = timer_started_at,
            activates_at      = timer_started_at
        WHERE lock_activated_at IS NULL
          AND status = 'pending'
        """
    ).rowcount
    print(f"[migrate] backfilled {n} pending row(s) as already-locked")

    conn.commit()

    # Post-state
    print("\n[migrate] post-state — rank_one_likes phase breakdown:")
    for row in cur.execute(
        """
        SELECT status,
               CASE
                 WHEN lock_activated_at IS NULL THEN 'grace'
                 ELSE 'locked'
               END AS phase,
               COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM rank_one_likes
        GROUP BY status, phase
        """
    ):
        print(f"  status={row[0]!r:>15}  phase={row[1]!r}  n={row[2]}")

    conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    db_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "DevDB.sqlite"
    migrate(db_path)

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import sqlite3, os
os.environ['DATABASE_URL'] = 'sqlite:////Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/DevDB.sqlite'

# direct sqlite insert
conn = sqlite3.connect("/Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/DevDB.sqlite")
cur = conn.cursor()
cur.execute("DELETE FROM rank_one_likes WHERE liker_id=2 AND status='pending'")
now_py = datetime.utcnow()
past_py = now_py - timedelta(seconds=60)
# Try BOTH formats: T-separator and space-separator
cur.execute("INSERT INTO rank_one_likes (liker_id, liked_id, timer_started_at, activates_at, lock_activated_at, expires_at, status) VALUES (2, 4, ?, ?, NULL, NULL, 'pending')", (now_py.isoformat(), past_py.isoformat()))
conn.commit()

# raw: what is stored
row = cur.execute("SELECT liker_id, liked_id, activates_at, typeof(activates_at) FROM rank_one_likes WHERE liker_id=2").fetchone()
print(f"raw sqlite: {row}")
conn.close()

# now via SQLAlchemy
from sqlalchemy import text
from models import SessionLocal, RankOneLike
db = SessionLocal()
try:
    r = db.query(RankOneLike).filter(RankOneLike.liker_id == 2).first()
    print(f"sqla: activates_at={r.activates_at!r} type={type(r.activates_at).__name__}")
    print(f"      lock_activated_at={r.lock_activated_at!r}")
    # try the actual filter
    n = datetime.utcnow()
    matched = db.query(RankOneLike).filter(
        RankOneLike.status == "pending",
        RankOneLike.lock_activated_at.is_(None),
        RankOneLike.activates_at <= n,
    ).count()
    print(f"      filter-count (activates_at <= now={n}): {matched}")
    # what SQL gets generated?
    from sqlalchemy.dialects import sqlite as sqlite_dialect
    q = db.query(RankOneLike).filter(
        RankOneLike.status == "pending",
        RankOneLike.lock_activated_at.is_(None),
        RankOneLike.activates_at <= n,
    )
    compiled = q.statement.compile(dialect=sqlite_dialect.dialect(), compile_kwargs={"literal_binds": True})
    print(f"SQL: {compiled}")
finally:
    db.close()

/usr/bin/env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin bash <<'COMMIT'
set -e
cd /tmp/m104-b
pkill -9 -f 'uvicorn.*main:app' 2>/dev/null || true

# remove the test heartbeat log we added (keep it — useful signal for ops)
# (actually, keep it — a zero-row run per 10s is cheap signal in prod)

git status --short
echo
git add models.py scheduler.py routes.py migrate_rank_one_grace.py
git diff --cached --stat
git commit -m "M104 Part B: rank-one two-phase grace->locked system

Schema (rank_one_likes):
- lock_activated_at TIMESTAMP NULL  (None => row is in GRACE phase)
- activates_at      TIMESTAMP NULL  (moment grace ends = next tick boundary)
- expires_at made nullable — now populated at activation, not creation

Migration (migrate_rank_one_grace.py, idempotent):
- Adds the two columns
- Rebuilds rank_one_likes to make expires_at nullable (SQLite limitation)
- Backfills existing pending rows as already-locked (lock_activated_at =
  timer_started_at, activates_at = timer_started_at) so the scheduler's
  expiry tick behaves as before for them.

Scheduler:
- rank_two_to_rank_one now creates rows in GRACE:
    activates_at       = _next_activation_utc(liker, now)
    lock_activated_at  = NULL
    expires_at         = NULL
- New tick_activation job (every 10s in dev, every 60s in prod):
    finds pending rows where lock_activated_at IS NULL AND activates_at <= now,
    sets lock_activated_at = now, expires_at = now + _timer_duration_for_speed
- Existing expire_* ticks unchanged (expires_at < now still filters out
  grace rows since NULL <= anything is NULL/falsy).

DTO + route updates (routes.py):
- RankOneLikeInfo gains activates_at, lock_activated_at, phase.
  Both expires_at and activates_at are now Optional[str].
- WhoILikeItem gains rank_one_phase + rank_one_activates_at +
  rank_one_expires_at (set only for the rank-1 item in the list).
- /rank-one-status filter: pending rows where lock_activated_at IS NULL
  OR expires_at > now. (Before: only 'expires_at > now' which hid grace.)
- /who_i_like: attaches the current rank-1 lock's phase to the rank-1
  item via a single extra query.
- unlike_user now wired as DELETE /api/like/{liked_user_id} (was a
  dangling undecorated function). Grace-phase rank-1 row is deleted
  alongside the Like; LOCKED rank-1 raises 409 so the FE surfaces the
  constraint instead of silently bypassing it.

Verified live:
- phase='grace' on fresh rank-1 creation; /api/rank-one-status returns
  expires_at=null, lock_activated_at=null.
- Wait past activates_at + one tick -> lock_activated_at set, expires_at
  populated, phase='locked'.
- DELETE /api/like/{id} -> 204 on grace, 409 on locked.
- Migration idempotent (3rd run no-op).

Not deployed — deploy gated on double-approval per prod-deploy memory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"

echo
git log --oneline -3
COMMIT

content.text:
---

[M104]

## Part A — merge train ✓

All four branches merged into `development` in the recommended order. Zero conflicts; each merge was `--no-ff` so history shows clear integration points. Every merge pushed immediately.

| Step | Commit | What landed |
|---|---|---|
| 1. M96 (api-referrals-me) | `829e9a3` | `/api/referrals/me` alias — 7-line routes.py diff |
| 2. M88 (backend-consolidated) | `3f76cda` | Photos pool split + `UserPhoto` deprecation + slot backfill |
| 3. M95 (env-config) | `af51a3f` | pydantic-settings, grouped config, ENVIRONMENT switch, auth-gated `/photos` in dev, CORS list |
| 4. M91+M97+M101 (gender/sex/pos + seed) | `8a3b8f6` | `position` column, new sex/sexuality enum keys, migration + seed_dev_clean.py with ranking backfill |

`fix/110-admin-panel` deliberately not merged (concurrent session still committing — last tip `235c062 M99panel1/P5`).

**Smoke tests against new development tip (port 8002):** all green.
```
/api/version 200 | /api/users/me 200 | /api/users 200 | /api/referrals/me 200 |
/api/me/referral-summary 200 | /api/who_i_like 200 | /api/who_likes_me 200 |
/photos noauth 401 | /photos auth 200
```

## Part B — rank-one two-phase on `fix/113-rank-one-grace` (commit `28325ae`, pushed)

Cut off new development tip. **All spec points implemented + live verified.**

### Schema (migrate_rank_one_grace.py — idempotent, rebuilds the table to make `expires_at` nullable since SQLite can't `ALTER` that in-place)

| Column | Type | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `lock_activated_at` | `TIMESTAMP NULL` | **None = grace phase**, non-null = locked |
| `activates_at` | `TIMESTAMP NULL` | Moment grace ends (next tick boundary) |
| `expires_at` | `TIMESTAMP NULL` (was NOT NULL) | Populated at activation, not creation |

Backfill: 44 existing pending rows marked as already-locked (`lock_activated_at = timer_started_at`) so the expire ticks keep behaving exactly as before for them. Idempotent: 3rd run is a full no-op.

### Scheduler

- `rank_two_to_rank_one`: new rows start in **grace**: `activates_at=_next_activation_utc(liker, now)`, `lock_activated_at=None`, `expires_at=None`.
- New `tick_activation` job: every 10 s in dev, every 60 s in prod. Filter = `pending AND lock_activated_at IS NULL AND activates_at <= now`. Flips row: `lock_activated_at=now`, `expires_at=now + _timer_duration_for_speed(speed)`. Bumps `bump_wil_version` per affected liker so the FE repaints.
- Existing `expire_rank_one_likes_*` ticks **unchanged** — `expires_at < now` naturally excludes grace rows (NULL comparisons fail in SQL).

### Route / DTO updates (`routes.py`)

- `RankOneLikeInfo`: adds `activates_at`, `lock_activated_at`, `phase`. `expires_at` now `Optional[str]`.
- `WhoILikeItem`: adds `rank_one_phase`, `rank_one_activates_at`, `rank_one_expires_at` — populated **only on the rank-1 item** in the list.
- `/api/rank-one-status` filter widened to `lock_activated_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now` so grace rows are visible.
- `/api/who_i_like` now fetches the single active rank-1 lock and decorates the rank-1 row with its phase + timing.
- **`DELETE /api/like/{liked_user_id}`** now wired (it was a dangling undecorated function). Grace-phase rank-1 row is deleted alongside the Like; **locked** row raises `409 Cannot unlike: rank-1 lock is active` so the FE surfaces the constraint instead of silently ghosting the request.

### Live verification (port 8004, real DevDB)

```
inserted grace row: activates_at = now - 30s
[tick_activation] run at 2026-04-24 12:47:22: found 1 row(s) to activate
[tick_activation] lock id=45 liker=2 -> LOCKED until 2026-04-25 12:47:22 (speed=1day)
[tick_activation] activated 1 row(s) at 2026-04-24 12:47:22
row state: liker=2 liked=4 locked=1 has_expiry=1   ✓ flipped
```

Plus:
- fresh grace row via API → `phase='grace'`, `expires_at=null`, `lock_activated_at=null`
- `DELETE /api/like/3` on grace row → `204` + row removed ✓
- `DELETE /api/like/15` on locked row → `409 Cannot unlike: rank-1 lock is active` ✓

### Debugging detour worth knowing about

First test runs silently failed because:

1. **Stale uvicorn** on port 8003 survived `lsof -ti:PORT | kill -9` (listener detection flaky under this harness). Fix: `pkill -9 -f 'uvicorn.*main:app'` + move to a fresh port. Worth baking into the reseed / test helpers.
2. **Stdout buffering** under `nohup` hid scheduler prints. Fix: `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1` + `python3 -u`. Should probably become the standard dev launch incantation.
3. **Datetime lex-mismatch**: my test rows inserted via raw `sqlite3` with `datetime.isoformat()` use `T` separator; SQLAlchemy's DateTime type writes `space` separator. Lexical comparison fails. Production code path never hits this because it always goes through SQLAlchemy. Flagging for anyone who writes direct-sqlite test harnesses.

## What's not in this commit (per spec)

- **Deploy.** Spec says "Deploy via double-approval." I didn't touch prod. When you're ready, the flow is: merge `fix/113-rank-one-grace` into `development` (trivial fast-forward expected), then `bash deploy/deploy.sh` from `development` per the deploy-workflow memory — that's your first approval; prod double-approval rule kicks in at actual deploy.
- **FE changes** (slide-left block on locked, countdown labels "Locks in HH:MM:SS" / "Expires in HH:MM:SS"). Backend exposes everything the FE needs via the new DTO fields; FE work is a separate fix branch.

## Final state

```
dev tip 8a3b8f6  — Merge fix/111-gender-sexuality-position
  merge 829e9a3  (M96)
  merge 3f76cda  (M88)
  merge af51a3f  (M95)
  merge 8a3b8f6  (M91/M97/M101)
fix/113-rank-one-grace 28325ae (M104 Part B) — cut off dev tip, pushed
fix/110-admin-panel    235c062 — untouched, concurrent session still working
tags: pre-merge-110-backend
```

## Rollback handle (if anything in the merge train goes sideways later)

```bash
git checkout development
git reset --hard pre-merge-110-backend        # only before next push
git push --force-with-lease origin development  # coordinate with concurrent session first
```

or per-commit:
```bash
git revert -m 1 <merge-commit-sha>
```

[M104]

Want me to `/schedule` an agent in ~1 week to check whether `fix/113-rank-one-grace` has merged + deployed, and open a cleanup PR if the scheduler's `tick_activation` is still emitting the "found 0 row(s)" heartbeat after a few days (safe to silence in prod once we're confident it's firing reliably)?

message.content:
[M107] HARD RULE: every feature cycle = one numbered cycle with matching branches.

Convention:
- fix/<N>-backend (or fix/<N>-<descriptor> if single BE branch per cycle)
- fix/<N>-frontend
- fix/<N>-admin-panel (if admin work in cycle)
- Exception: if cycle is backend-only or frontend-only, the counterpart can be skipped BUT the number must never be reused for a different feature.

NEVER:
- Do not split a cycle number into multiple BE branches (fix/112-env-config + fix/112-api-referrals-me was wrong — should have been fix/112-backend with both changes, OR cycle 112 = one thing + cycle 113 = other thing).
- Do not use inconsistent names (fix/113-rank-one-grace should have been fix/113-backend).
- Do not branch off random tips.

WHEN IN DOUBT: ask user for clarification before creating a branch.

PREVIOUS VIOLATIONS:
- Cycle 112: split into fix/112-env-config + fix/112-api-referrals-me (both merged, no harm BUT set precedent)
- Cycle 113: fix/113-rank-one-grace (naming inconsistent — should be fix/113-backend)

Going forward: strict one-number-one-pair convention.

Save as memory: workflow_branch_naming.md

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [M107].

input.content:
---
name: Branch naming — one number, one pair, never split
description: Every feature cycle = one number N with matching fix/<N>-backend, fix/<N>-frontend, fix/<N>-admin-panel branches. Never split a cycle number across multiple backend branches; never reuse N for a different feature.
type: feedback
---

Every feature cycle gets ONE number `<N>`. Branches in that cycle must use the canonical per-role names:

- `fix/<N>-backend` — BackA20 work (or `fix/<N>-<descriptor>` only if there's a single branch in that cycle and no other role is involved)
- `fix/<N>-frontend` — FE app work
- `fix/<N>-admin-panel` — admin panel work (only if the cycle touches admin)

### Hard rules

- **Never split one cycle across multiple backend branches.** Cycle 112 was split into `fix/112-env-config` + `fix/112-api-referrals-me` — that was wrong. The right call was either: one `fix/112-backend` with both changes, OR make them cycles 112 and 113 (one feature each).
- **Never reuse a cycle number for a different feature,** even if the counterpart branch was skipped. If cycle 114 was backend-only, cycle 115 can have BE+FE; 114 stays frozen.
- **Never branch off a random tip.** Always cut from `development` tip at cycle start (or from an explicitly-named base for cherry-picks — and document it in the commit).
- **Naming must be consistent.** `fix/113-rank-one-grace` was wrong — should have been `fix/113-backend`. Descriptors are OK only when no counterpart exists; as soon as a second role joins the cycle, both branches get role-based names.
- **When in doubt, ASK the user before creating a branch.** A wrong branch name is cheap to avoid up-front and expensive to unwind after commits land.

### Past violations (do not repeat)

- **Cycle 112**: split into `fix/112-env-config` + `fix/112-api-referrals-me`. Both merged, no harm, but set a bad precedent.
- **Cycle 113**: used `fix/113-rank-one-grace` descriptor instead of `fix/113-backend`.

**Why:** User established this convention M107 (2026-04-24) after noticing the cycle-112 split and cycle-113 naming drift. The goal is unambiguous cross-role references: when the user says "cycle 115", there is exactly one BE branch and at most one FE branch and at most one admin-panel branch, each with a predictable name.

**How to apply:** Before `git checkout -b` on any fix branch, confirm the cycle number the user has in mind and use the role-based name. If I think a change belongs in the current cycle but the user hasn't said so, ask (e.g. "wire this into fix/115-backend, or cut fix/116 for it?"). If I think the scope warrants a new cycle, ask before minting one. For backend-only or frontend-only cycles, the counterpart branch is skipped — but the number is frozen to that cycle forever.

content.text:
---

[M107]

Saved. Memory captures the rule with explicit examples of what was wrong (cycles 112 split, 113 descriptor) and the strict "ask before branching" clause — so future-me defers to you when there's ambiguity about which cycle something belongs to, rather than minting a branch and setting another precedent.

`MEMORY.md` index updated right under the `feedback_git_workflow` entry so the branch-naming rule surfaces alongside the broader fix-branch workflow.

Two small clarifiers worth confirming so I don't guess later — happy to treat these as "ask the user" moments too, but if you have a standing answer:

1. **Renaming past branches.** `fix/113-rank-one-grace` is already pushed + has `M104 Part B` on it. Want me to rename it to `fix/113-backend` (local rename + force-push to new remote ref + delete old remote) at some point, or leave the history as documented evidence of the prior convention?
2. **Cycle boundaries when things are "one big thing in two passes".** E.g. if FE needs a schema change I already shipped in cycle 113's backend, but I only notice after cycle 113 is closed — is that a new cycle (114), or is cycle 113 "reopened" with a follow-up `fix/113-backend` picking up extra commits? My read of the rule says new cycle 114 — N is frozen.

[M107]

content.text:
Interim report while the build runs — scheduled a check-back in 2 min to confirm app launch.

---

[M108] — **interim**

All prep done. Waiting on `flutter run` iOS build.

| Step | Result |
|---|---|
| 1. Kill :8088 + :8000 | ✓ both cleared (+ pkilled stragglers) |
| 2a. BackA20 → `development`, `git pull` | ✓ tip `8a3b8f6 Merge fix/111-gender-sexuality-position` |
| 2b. `./venv/bin/python3 main.py` backgrounded | ✓ pid `47910` running |
| 2c. `GET /api/version` | ✓ HTTP 200 → `{"version":"2026-04-23(109.1)"}` |
| | scheduler: `expire_rank_one_likes_dev`, `create_rank_one_likes`, `tick_tz_aware_activations`, `tick_login_rate_limit_alerts` |
| 3. Boot iPhone 17 sim (`841E07...CDFC`) | already booted; `open -a Simulator` → foregrounded |
| 4a. FrontA20 already on `fix/111-frontend`; `git pull` | ✓ up to date |
| 4b. `flutter clean` + `flutter pub get` | ✓ |
| 4c. `flutter run` | in flight — xcodebuild PID `48377` compiling, ~60s elapsed so far |

### Heads-up on concurrent sim session

There's a second `flutter run` (PID `23770`) from a different session — it already deployed a `Runner.app` from some other branch and has a live debug session attached (VM service on `:53112`). When my `--no-hot` build completes, it'll overwrite that `Runner.app` with the `fix/111-frontend` binary. If the concurrent session was testing something there, they'll get their app swapped under them. Flagging since the branch-naming memory warns about stepping on concurrent work.

### Version gap worth knowing

Development has M88+M95+M96+M91+M97+M101, but **not** M104 Part B (rank-one grace). The scheduler started without `tick_activation` — grace-phase fields on `/api/rank-one-status` / `/api/who_i_like` won't be populated. If the Build 111 FE expects them, rows will read as legacy "locked from creation" behavior. Not an error per se, just a product-level difference.

### What I'm waiting on

`/tmp/flutter-run.log` currently has: `Launching lib/main.dart ... Running pod install ... Running Xcode build ...`. I've scheduled a check-back at 14:36 local (2 min) — if it's up and running by then I'll confirm with a final state; if build failed I'll paste the error. Backend log is already streaming `[CRON DEV] expire_rank_one_likes_dev` every 10s, so the backend is alive and ticking.

Reproduce the backend side from scratch yourself:
```bash
cd /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20
lsof -ti :8000 2>/dev/null | xargs -r kill -9
PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 nohup ./venv/bin/python3 -u main.py > backend.log 2>&1 & echo $! > backend.pid
sleep 3 && curl -s localhost:8000/api/version
```

[M108 — interim, will finalize once flutter run attaches or errors]

content.text:
Build succeeded. App is running and auto-logged in as Jason on the simulator.

---

[M108] — **final**

Full boot is live. Build 111 FE is attached to the local backend and fetching data.

| Stage | State |
|---|---|
| Backend | ✓ `localhost:8000` serving, pid `47910`, env=dev, scheduler running 4 jobs |
| `/api/version` | ✓ 200 → `{"version":"2026-04-23(109.1)"}` |
| Simulator | ✓ iPhone 17 (`841E07...CDFC`) booted, Simulator.app foregrounded |
| `flutter run` | ✓ **Xcode build done (53.4 s)**, Dart VM Service at `http://127.0.0.1:54288/` |
| App process | ✓ `Runner.app` pid `50933` running on sim |
| Auto-login | ✓ POST `/api/login` → 200, Jason token issued |
| First API call | ✓ GET `/api/rank-one-status` → 200 with two active rank-1 entries (`as_liker=fiercefrost`, `as_liked=silvercomet`) |
| Timer widget | ✓ `DEBUG rankOne built: Instance of '_RankOneTimer'` — UI rendered |

### Observations

- **Expected FE/backend field mismatch (not an error).** The `rank-one-status` response is the **pre-M104 shape** (no `phase`, no `activates_at`, no `lock_activated_at`). That's correct — development doesn't have fix/113-rank-one-grace merged yet. The FE parses legacy fields fine and treats everything as locked-from-creation. If Build 111 was spec'd against the two-phase DTO, you'd see timer labels say "Expires in…" always (no "Locks in…"); if it was spec'd against legacy, you're in the happy path.
- **TZ math sanity-checks.** Jason's 1-day `expires_at = 2026-04-24T20:00:00Z` = 21:00 in Europe/London (BST = UTC+1). Matches the scheduler's "next 21:00 local" rule.
- **Concurrent flutter process** (PID `23770`, earlier session's) is still running separately — pointing at the same simulator so my install at `01AC8502-FD9E-4D33-8A5F-285AF32CB57C` has replaced their earlier `1A930EDE-2279-4AFA-B03C-DDB9B353471F`. Their VM service at `:53112` is likely orphaned. If that session was mid-test, they'll want to relaunch.

### Running things right now

```
pid 47910 — backend on :8000 (PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1, logs → backend.log)
pid 48257 — flutter_tools run (controlling the debug session)
pid 50933 — Runner.app on sim
```

### Ignore the upcoming wake-up

I scheduled a 2-min check-back earlier. It'll fire at ~14:36 local and deliver a "continue M108" prompt to me. Build already completed so that tick will be a no-op; you can ignore any extra text it produces (or I'll just confirm state again briefly).

### Teardown when you're done testing

```bash
# kill sim app + flutter controller
kill 48257 50933 2>/dev/null

# kill backend
kill "$(cat /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/backend.pid)"
rm -f /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20/backend.pid

# (optional) shut down the sim
xcrun simctl shutdown 841E07BF-ABB0-4FB2-8D01-E7CC0648CDFC
```

message.content:
[M110] Bug J investigation — WIL unlike returns 404.

Branch: new fix/114-backend off development tip. Banner bump not needed for backend.

1. Reproduce:
   TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/api/login -d 'username=Jason&password=111111111111' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' | python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["access_token"])')
   curl -v -X DELETE localhost:8000/api/like/3 -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
2. If 404 — check routes.py for DELETE /api/like/{liked_user_id} definition
3. Note: M104 Part B (rank-one grace) ADDED the DELETE endpoint as part of grace-unlike logic. fix/113-rank-one-grace has it. development (merge train) may NOT have it because fix/113 isn't merged yet.
4. If missing on development: cherry-pick the DELETE endpoint from fix/113-rank-one-grace onto fix/114-backend — just the endpoint, not the scheduler/schema changes.
5. Or if DELETE already exists and returning 404 for other reason, fix that.
6. Verify + commit + push.

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [M110].

content.text: Executing. Setting up fix/114-backend worktree, reproducing the 404, adding the minimal decorator, then verifying.

async def unlike_user(
    liked_user_id: int,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Remove a like and resequence the personal like_rank for remaining likes."""
    liked_user = db.query(User).filter(User.id == liked_user_id).first()

    # Remove Like row
    like = db.query(Like).filter(
        Like.liker_id == current_user.id,
        Like.liked_id == liked_user_id,

content.text:
---

[M110]

## Bug J — diagnosed + fixed

**Root cause:** `routes.py:948` defines `async def unlike_user(...)` but had **no `@router.delete` decorator**. The function body was correct (Like + UserLikeStats + Match cleanup + rank resequence), but without the decorator the route was never registered — `DELETE /api/like/{id}` 404'd.

M104 Part B (on `fix/113-rank-one-grace`) had already fixed this AND layered grace-phase logic on top. Since fix/113 isn't merged yet — and its added logic references `lock_activated_at` which doesn't exist in development's schema — the minimal surgical fix on cycle 114 is just the decorator.

Per M107: `fix/114-backend` cut off `development` tip (`8a3b8f6`). No descriptor, role-based name.

## Change (commit `2f24991`, pushed)

```python
+@router.delete("/like/{liked_user_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
 async def unlike_user(
     liked_user_id: int,
     current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
     db: Session = Depends(get_db),
 ):
```

Plus a short docstring explaining the M104 Part B relationship so nobody thinks to re-wire it when fix/113 merges (the merge will bring grace-aware replacement automatically).

## Verification (fresh backend on :8000)

```
DELETE /api/like/3  (auth, Like exists)        HTTP 204  ✓ Like + ranking row deleted
DELETE /api/like/3  (auth, row already gone)   HTTP 204  ✓ idempotent
DELETE /api/like/3  (no auth)                  HTTP 401  ✓ bearer gate fires
/api/version                                   HTTP 200
/api/who_i_like  (Jason)                       HTTP 200
OpenAPI delete routes now: /api/like/{liked_user_id}, /api/block/{user_id}, /api/gallery/{slot}
```

## Incidents worth flagging for the workflow-rules memory

Two things went wrong before the fix actually ran:

1. **HEAD silently moved from `development` to `fix/111-gender-sexuality-position`** between my `git branch --show-current` read (saw `development`) and my `git checkout -b fix/114-backend`. Reflog confirms it — the concurrent admin-panel session (M99panel7/P1-P5) was active and flipped branches in the shared working tree. My `fix/114-backend` was initially cut off fix/111's tip (`8c61edc`), not development (`8a3b8f6`). I caught it via `git rev-parse HEAD` vs `development` and hard-reset before any commit. **Signal for future work: always re-confirm HEAD immediately before `checkout -b` when a concurrent session is known active.**
2. **Stale backend on :8000.** `pkill -f 'python.*main\.py'` didn't catch pid `59738` (shell-history command didn't match the regex for some reason), my first restart of the new code silently died with `Errno 48: address already in use`, so my first `DELETE /api/like/3` kept hitting the old backend. Fix: direct `kill -9 <pid>` after `lsof -iTCP:8000 -sTCP:LISTEN`. **Signal: always confirm the running pid matches the one you just started before curl-testing.**

Both cost a few minutes; both are automatable into a pre-flight helper if this keeps happening.

## State

- `fix/114-backend` 2f24991 — pushed, ready to merge
- Backend on :8000 pid `60271`, running fix/114 code
- Simulator app (PID `50933` from M108 earlier) is likely still up but pointing at the old backend instance; FE users should reload / relaunch to see the 204 instead of 404

## Unblock Build 111 FE

Tell the simulator app to retry an unlike — should now 204 cleanly.

```bash
# quick re-verify locally with the FE flow (from the simulator's POV):
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/api/login -d 'username=Jason&password=111111111111' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' | ./venv/bin/python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["access_token"])')
curl -v -X DELETE localhost:8000/api/like/3 -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"  # 204
```

[M110]

content: Merge train landed on development; just shipped fix/114-backend to unblock the FE unlike 404. Next: test the DELETE from the simulator app, then merge fix/114-backend into development.

[M114] Backend support for Build 114 frontend features (F19 + F22).

Branch: fix/114-backend (same as M110).

F19 — Discover filter params for /api/users
1. Audit current /api/users query params (sex, sexuality, photo_type, etc)
2. Add/extend:
   - sex=csv (already exists? verify)
   - sexuality=csv
   - position=csv
3. Validate against VALID_SEX_VALUES, VALID_SEXUALITY_VALUES, VALID_POSITION_VALUES
4. 422 on invalid with M63-style error body

F22 — position_visible column + visibility logic
5. Add User.position_visible = Column(Boolean, nullable=False, default=True)
6. Migration: all existing users default to True
7. /api/users and /api/users/{id}: include position only if position_visible=True AND requester is in matches
   - Self-view (requester == target): always include position
   - Matched peer: include if position_visible
   - Unmatched peer: omit position field
8. PUT /users/me: accept position_visible

Commit + push fix/114-backend.

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [M114].

file.content:
class UserListItem(BaseModel):
    id: int
    username: str
    age: Optional[int] = None
    sex: Optional[str] = None
    sexuality: Optional[str] = None
    bio: Optional[str] = None
    headline: Optional[str] = None
    height: Optional[int] = None
    star_sign: Optional[str] = None
    photo_url: Optional[str] = None
    body_photo_url: Optional[str] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class UsersListResponse(BaseModel):
    users: List[UserListItem]
    total: int
    page: int
    per_page: int
    has_more: bool


@router.get("/users", response_model=UsersListResponse)
async def list_users(
    page: int = 1,
    per_page: int = 30,
    sex: Optional[str] = None,
    sexuality: Optional[str] = None,
    age_min: Optional[int] = None,
    age_max: Optional[int] = None,
    photo_type: Optional[str] = None,
    current_user: Optional[User] = Depends(get_optional_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Return a paginated list of users, excluding the authenticated user."""
    query = db.query(User)
    if current_user is not None:
        # Exclude self
        query = query.filter(User.id != current_user.id)
        # Exclude users I've blocked
        from sqlalchemy import select, exists, or_
        blocked_by_me = select(Block.blocked_id).where(Block.blocker_id == current_user.id).scalar_subquery()
        # Exclude users who have blocked me
        blocked_me = select(Block.blocker_id).where(Block.blocked_id == current_user.id).scalar_subquery()
        # Exclude users I've already liked
        query = query.filter(User.id.notin_(blocked_by_me)).filter(User.id.notin_(blocked_me)).filter(~exists().where((Like.liker_id == current_user.id) & (Like.liked_id == User.id)))
        # Exclude users who've already liked me (they belong in WLM, not Discover)
        query = query.filter(
            ~exists().where((Like.liker_id == User.id) & (Like.liked_id == current_user.id))
        )
        # Exclude users I'm matched with (either direction) — they belong in Matches
        query = query.filter(
            ~exists().where(
                or_(
                    (Match.user1_id == current_user.id) & (Match.user2_id == User.id),
                    (Match.user2_id == current_user.id) & (Match.user1_id == User.id),
                )
            )
        )
        # Dating speed tier filter — only show users with the same dating_speed
        my_speed = current_user.dating_speed or '1day'
        query = query.filter((User.dating_speed == my_speed) | ((User.dating_speed.is_(None)) & (my_speed == '1day')))
        # Timezone filter — only show users in the same TZ. NULL is treated
        # as 'Europe/London' on both sides (per M34-reply Q5).
        my_tz = current_user.timezone or 'Europe/London'
        query = query.filter((User.timezone == my_tz) | ((User.timezone.is_(None)) & (my_tz == 'Europe/London')))
    # Search filters
    if sex:
        query = query.filter(User.sex == sex)
    if sexuality:
        query = query.filter(User.sexuality == sexuality)
    if age_min is not None:
        query = query.filter(User.age >= age_min)
    if age_max is not None:
        query = query.filter(User.age <= age_max)
    if photo_type is not None:
        _validate_enum(photo_type, {"face", "body_photo"}, "photo_type")
        from sqlalchemy import exists as sa_exists, or_ as sa_or
        if photo_type == "body_photo":
            query = query.filter(
                sa_exists().where(
                    (UserMedia.user_id == User.id) & (UserMedia.slot == "full_length")
                )
            )
        else:  # "face"
            # Face photo comes from EITHER UserPhoto (legacy, face-slot double)
            # OR UserMedia slot='face' (new gallery path).
            query = query.filter(
                sa_or(
                    sa_exists().where(
                        (UserPhoto.user_id == User.id)
                        & (UserPhoto.photo_filename.isnot(None))
                    ),
                    sa_exists().where(
                        (UserMedia.user_id == User.id) & (UserMedia.slot == "face")
                    ),
                )

            body_photo_url=bp.get(u.id),
        ))
    return UsersListResponse(
        users=items,
        total=total,
        page=page,
        per_page=per_page,
        has_more=(offset + per_page) < total,
    )

class LikeRequest(BaseModel):
    liked_user_id: int

class BlockRequest(BaseModel):
    blocked_user_id: int


# ── Personal rank helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _recalculate_user_ranks(db: Session, liker_username: str) -> None:
    """Reassign like_rank 1..N sequentially for all of a user's likes,
    preserving the current order (ordered by existing rank, then id)."""
    rows = (
        db.query(UserLikeStats)
        .filter(UserLikeStats.username == liker_username)
        .order_by(
            UserLikeStats.like_rank.asc().nullslast(),
            UserLikeStats.id.asc(),
        )
        .all()
    )
    for i, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
        row.like_rank = i
    db.commit()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
M114 (F22): add users.position_visible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1.

Idempotent — no-op if the column already exists.

Usage:
    ./venv/bin/python3 migrate_position_visible.py [db_path]
"""
import sqlite3
import sys


def column_exists(cur, table, col):
    return col in {row[1] for row in cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})").fetchall()}


def migrate(db_path: str):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cur = conn.cursor()

    if column_exists(cur, "users", "position_visible"):
        print("[migrate] users.position_visible already exists — skipping")
    else:
        # DEFAULT 1 populates existing rows at column-add time, so we don't
        # need a separate UPDATE backfill.
        cur.execute("ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN position_visible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1")
        print("[migrate] added users.position_visible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1")

    conn.commit()

    # Post-state
    print("\n[migrate] post-state — position_visible distribution:")
    for val, n in cur.execute(
        "SELECT position_visible, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY position_visible"
    ):
        print(f"  position_visible={val}: {n}")
    conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    migrate(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "DevDB.sqlite")

from datetime import datetime
from sqlalchemy import Boolean, Column, DateTime, Integer, String, Text, Date, create_engine, ForeignKey, UniqueConstraint
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from config import DATABASE_URL

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False})
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

Base = declarative_base()


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    username = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True)
    hashed_password = Column(String)
    date_of_birth = Column(Date, nullable=True)
    age = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    # M91 (2026-04-24): stable internal keys, FE renders display labels+emojis.
    sex = Column(String, nullable=True)  # male/female/trans_unspecified/trans_eggplant/trans_clam, NULL allowed
    sexuality = Column(String, nullable=True)  # straight/other, NULL allowed
    bio = Column(String, nullable=True)
    headline = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    height = Column(Integer, nullable=True)  # cm
    star_sign = Column(String, nullable=True)
    dating_speed = Column(String, default='1day')  # '1min', '1hour', '1day', '1week'
    timezone = Column(String, default='Europe/London', nullable=True)
    last_lock_activation = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    referred_by = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=True, index=True)
    # M91: position preference. Conditional on sex at the FE; backend only validates allowed values.
    position = Column(String, nullable=True)  # top/both_more_top/both/both_more_bottom/bottom, NULL allowed

    photo = relationship("UserPhoto", back_populates="user", uselist=False)


# DEPRECATED (M88): superseded by UserMedia(slot='face'). Retained for back-compat
# reads in routes.py (/users/me, /users/{id}, browse, upload). Do not add new
# callers. Plan to migrate readers and drop this table in a future fix.
class UserPhoto(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_photos"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), unique=True, nullable=False)
    photo_filename = Column(String, nullable=True)  # stored filename e.g. "photoface_<user_id>.jpg"
    photo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)        # original source URL

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="photo")


class Like(Base):
    __tablename__ = "likes"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("liker_id", "liked_id", name="uq_like_pair"),)
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    voice_note = Column(String, nullable=True)  # filename e.g. "like_123_456.m4a"


class Block(Base):
    __tablename__ = "blocks"
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    blocker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    blocked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    # nullable so the prod migration succeeds without backfill; new rows
    # get stamped via the python-side default on insert.
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=True)


class UserLikeStats(Base):
    """Personal like ranking — one row per (liker, liked) pair.

    username  : the user doing the liking
    user_likes: the user being liked
    like_rank : 1-based position in THIS user's personal liked list (1 = top)
    """
    __tablename__ = "senders_like_ranking"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("username", "user_likes", name="uq_senders_like_ranking_pair"),)

    id         = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username   = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    user_likes = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    like_rank  = Column(Integer, nullable=True)


class Match(Base):
    __tablename__ = "matches"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user1_id", "user2_id", name="uq_match_pair"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True, autoincrement=True)
    user1_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    user2_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Message(Base):
    __tablename__ = "messages"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    sender_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    receiver_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    content = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)
    is_read = Column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)


class RankOneLike(Base):
    __tablename__ = "rank_one_likes"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    liker_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    liked_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    timer_started_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="pending", nullable=False)  # pending, matched, like_expired


class ReferrerEvent(Base):
    __tablename__ = "referrer_events"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    event_name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    value = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=0)
    referrer_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), index=True, nullable=False)
    referred_username = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.username"), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class UserMedia(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user_media"
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user_id", "slot", name="uq_user_media_slot"),)

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    slot = Column(String, nullable=False)  # face, full_length, happy, funny, hobby, video
    filename = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)


class Feedback(Base):
    __tablename__ = "feedback"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    # nullable so an anonymous-feedback flow can be added later without
    # another migration; for now the POST endpoint requires auth.
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True, index=True)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    screenshot_path = Column(String, nullable=True)
    app_version = Column(String, nullable=False)
    platform = Column(String, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String, default="new", nullable=False)  # new | read | resolved
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False, index=True)


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

def _validate_enum(value, allowed, field_name):
    if value is not None and value not in allowed:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail=f"{field_name} must be one of: {sorted(allowed)}",
        )


def _parse_csv_enum(raw, allowed, field_name):
    """M114 F19: parse comma-separated query param into a list of enum values.
    Validates each value; raises 422 with a FastAPI-shaped detail list on any
    invalid member. Returns None when raw is None/empty (caller skips filter).

    Examples:
        sex=male         -> ['male']
        sex=male,female  -> ['male', 'female']

        sex=male,female  -> ['male', 'female']
        sex=              -> None (treated as "no filter")
        sex=Male         -> 422 (legacy capitalized rejected since M91)

    return values

class UserListItem(BaseModel):
    id: int
    username: str
    age: Optional[int] = None
    sex: Optional[str] = None
    sexuality: Optional[str] = None
    # M114 F22: present only when visibility rule allows (self-view OR matched peer w/ visibility on).
    position: Optional[str] = None
    bio: Optional[str] = None
    headline: Optional[str] = None
    height: Optional[int] = None
    star_sign: Optional[str] = None
    photo_url: Optional[str] = None
    body_photo_url: Optional[str] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


def _is_matched(db: Session, a_id: int, b_id: int) -> bool:
    """Return True if users a and b have an active Match row (either pair ordering)."""
    if a_id == b_id:
        return False
    u1, u2 = min(a_id, b_id), max(a_id, b_id)
    return db.query(Match).filter(Match.user1_id == u1, Match.user2_id == u2).first() is not None


def _position_for_requester(db: Session, requester_id: Optional[int], target: User) -> Optional[str]:
    """M114 F22 visibility gate.

    Returns target.position iff:
      - requester == target (self-view), OR
      - target.position_visible AND requester is matched with target
    Otherwise returns None (FE treats as "not disclosed").
    Unauthenticated (requester_id is None) -> None.
    """
    if requester_id is None or target.position is None:
        return None
    if requester_id == target.id:
        return target.position
    if not getattr(target, "position_visible", True):
        return None
    return target.position if _is_matched(db, requester_id, target.id) else None

cd /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20
/usr/sbin/lsof -ti:8000 -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null | xargs -r kill -9
sleep 1
rm -f backend.log
PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 nohup ./venv/bin/python3 -u main.py > backend.log 2>&1 &
echo $! > backend.pid
sleep 4
echo "pid: $(cat backend.pid)"
head -8 backend.log

TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/api/login -d 'username=Jason&password=111111111111' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' | ./venv/bin/python3 -c 'import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["access_token"])')
echo "token: ${TOKEN:0:20}..."
echo
echo '=== F19: CSV filters ==='
for q in 'sex=male' 'sex=male,female' 'sex=male,female&sexuality=straight' 'position=top,both,bottom' 'sex=Male' 'position=versatile'; do
    code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/f19.json -w '%{http_code}' "localhost:8000/api/users?$q" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN")
    printf '  ?%-45s HTTP %s ' "$q" "$code"
    if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
        ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/f19.json')); print(f'→ {d[\"total\"]} users')"
    elif [ "$code" = "422" ]; then
        ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/f19.json')); det=d.get('detail',[{}])[0]; print(f'→ 422: {det.get(\"msg\",\"?\")}')"
    else
        echo ''
    fi
done

echo
echo '=== F22: position_visible on self-view ==='
curl -s localhost:8000/api/users/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'  position={d.get(\"position\")!r} position_visible={d.get(\"position_visible\")}')"

echo
echo '=== F22: PUT /users/me set position_visible=false ==='
curl -s -X PUT localhost:8000/api/users/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"position_visible": false}' | ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'  after PUT: position_visible={d.get(\"position_visible\")}')"

echo
echo '=== F22: /api/users/{id} for an unmatched peer (position must be null) ==='
curl -s "localhost:8000/api/users/3" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'  user id=3 position={d.get(\"position\")!r}  (expect null — unmatched)')"

echo
echo '=== F22: create a Match between Jason (1) and user 3, re-check ==='
sqlite3 DevDB.sqlite "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO matches (user1_id, user2_id) VALUES (1, 3);"
curl -s "localhost:8000/api/users/3" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'  user id=3 (now matched) position={d.get(\"position\")!r}')"

echo
echo '=== F22: user 3 hides position, Jason re-checks ==='
sqlite3 DevDB.sqlite "UPDATE users SET position_visible=0 WHERE id=3;"
curl -s "localhost:8000/api/users/3" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | ./venv/bin/python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'  user id=3 (matched, hidden) position={d.get(\"position\")!r}  (expect null)')"

echo
echo '=== F22: restore state ==='
sqlite3 DevDB.sqlite "UPDATE users SET position_visible=1 WHERE id=3; DELETE FROM matches WHERE user1_id=1 AND user2_id=3;"
curl -s -X PUT localhost:8000/api/users/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"position_visible": true}' > /dev/null
echo '  restored'

echo
echo '=== /api/users/{id} 404 for missing ==='
printf '  GET /api/users/99999 HTTP '
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' localhost:8000/api/users/99999 -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

echo
echo '=== unauth /api/users/{id} ==='
printf '  GET /api/users/3 (noauth) HTTP '
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' localhost:8000/api/users/3

content.text: All three visibility branches correct. Commit + push.

input.command:
cd /Users/macminiai/projects/BackA20
git status --short | grep -v '^??'
echo
git add models.py routes.py migrate_position_visible.py
git diff --cached --stat
echo
git commit -m "M114: F19 CSV discover filters + F22 position visibility gate

F19 — /api/users query params now accept CSV lists
- sex / sexuality / position each take comma-separated values
  (e.g. sex=male,female). Single values keep working.
- _parse_csv_enum() parses + validates each member against the
  existing VALID_*_VALUES sets.
- Invalid members return 422 with FastAPI-shaped detail list:
    {\"detail\": [{\"type\":\"enum\", \"loc\":[\"query\",\"sex\"],
                 \"msg\":\"...\", \"input\":\"Male\",
                 \"ctx\":{\"expected\":[\"female\",\"male\",...]}}]}
- SQL uses User.<col>.in_(list) instead of == so single+multi paths
  share the same code.

F22 — position privacy gate
- User.position_visible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE added to
  models.User; migrate_position_visible.py idempotently applies
  the column (SQLite DEFAULT populates existing rows).
- _position_for_requester(db, requester_id, target) returns
  target.position iff self-view OR (target.position_visible AND
  requester is in matches); else None.
- UserListItem now has Optional[position] gated via the helper in:
  - GET /api/users (browse)
  - GET /api/users/{user_id}  ← NEW endpoint (was absent; FE profile
    screen needed it)
- ProfileResponse exposes position_visible on self-view.
- PUT /users/me accepts position_visible bool.

Verified: all three visibility branches
  matched + visible=True  -> \"top\"
  matched + visible=False -> null
  unmatched               -> null
plus self-view always carries position, PUT toggles visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git push origin fix/114-backend
echo
git log --oneline -3
